subject: Math
description: Determine whether a proportional relationship exists between two quantities, when given a table of values, context, or graph.
description_plain: Your child can tell if two quantities have a proportional relationship by looking at tables, stories, or graphs.

subject: Math
description: Determine a missing value in a ratio table that represents a proportional relationship between two quantities using a unit rate.
description_plain: Your child can find a missing number in a ratio table by using the unit rate.

subject: Math
description: Identify the unit rate of a proportional relationship represented by a table of values, a contextual situation, or a graph.
description_plain: Your child can find the unit rate from a table, story, or graph that shows a proportional relationship.

subject: Math
description: The student will identify and represent proportional relationships between two quantities, including those in context (unit rates are limited to positive values).
description_plain: Your child can identify and show proportional relationships between two amounts, including finding unit rates (only positive numbers).

subject: Math
description: Create a table of equivalent ratios to represent a proportional relationship between two quantities, when given a contextual situation.
description_plain: Your child can make a table of equivalent ratios from a real-life situation showing a proportional relationship.

subject: Math
description: Create a table of equivalent ratios to represent a proportional relationship between two quantities, when given a ratio.
description_plain: Your child can make a table of equivalent ratios when given a ratio.

subject: Math
description: Create a relationship in words for a given ratio expressed symbolically.
description_plain: Your child can explain in words what a given ratio means.

subject: Math
description: Represent different comparisons within the same quantity or between different quantities (e.g., part to part, part to whole, whole to whole).
description_plain: Your child can show different types of comparisons, like part to part or part to whole.

subject: Math
description: Represent a relationship in context that makes a comparison by using the notations 𝑎 𝑏 , a:b, and a to b.
description_plain: Your child can write a comparison using different ratio symbols like a/b, a:b, or 'a to b' in a real-life context.

subject: Math
description: Represent a relationship between two quantities using ratios.
description_plain: Your child can show how two amounts are related using ratios.

subject: Math
description: The student will use ratios to represent relationships between quantities, including those in context.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios to show relationships between amounts, including real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Observe patterns in data to identify outliers and determine their effect on mean, median, mode, or range.
description_plain: Your child can find unusual data points and explain how they change the average, middle, most common value, or range.

subject: Math
description: Determine the effect on measures of center when a single value of a data set is added, removed, or changed.
description_plain: Your child can explain how adding, removing, or changing one data point affects the average or middle value.

subject: Math
description: Represent the mean of a set of data graphically as the balance point represented in a line plot (dot plot).
description_plain: Your child can show the average of data on a dot plot as the point where the data balances.

subject: Math
description: The student will represent the mean as a balance point and determine the effect on statistical measures when a data point is added, removed, or changed.
description_plain: Your child can show the average as a balance point and explain how changing data points affects statistics.

subject: Math
description: Compare data represented in a circle graph with the same data represented in other graphs, including but not limited to bar graphs, pictographs, and line plots (dot plots), and justify which graphical representation best represents the data.
description_plain: Your child can look at the same data shown in a circle graph and other types of graphs, like bar graphs or pictographs, and explain which graph shows the data best.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data represented in a circle graph by making observations and drawing conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can study a circle graph, notice important details, and make conclusions based on what they see.

subject: Math
description: Organize and represent data using circle graphs, with and without the use of technology tools. The number of data values should be limited to allow for comparisons that have denominators of 12 or less or those that are factors of 100 (e.g., in a class of 20 students, 7 choose apples as a favorite fruit, so the comparison is 7 out of 20, 7 20 , or 35%).
description_plain: Your child can organize data and create circle graphs by hand or using technology, using numbers that make it easy to compare parts of the whole.

subject: Math
description: Determine the factors that will ensure that the data collected is a sample that is representative of a larger population.
description_plain: Your child understands how to choose data that fairly represents a larger group when collecting information.

subject: Math
description: Determine the data needed to answer a formulated question and collect the data (or acquire existing data) using various methods (e.g., observations, measurement, surveys, experiments).
description_plain: Your child can decide what data is needed to answer a question and collect it by watching, measuring, surveying, or experimenting.

subject: Math
description: Formulate questions that require the collection or acquisition of data with a focus on circle graphs.
description_plain: Your child can create questions that need data collection, especially questions that can be answered using circle graphs.

subject: Math
description: The student will apply the data cycle (formulate questions; collect or acquire data; organize and represent data; and analyze data and communicate results) with a focus on circle graphs.
description_plain: Your child will practice the steps of working with data: asking questions, gathering data, organizing it, and understanding it, focusing on circle graphs.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether polygons are congruent or noncongruent according to the measures of their sides and angles.
description_plain: Your child can tell if shapes are exactly the same or different by looking at their side lengths and angle sizes.

subject: Math
description: Determine the congruence of segments, angles, and polygons given their properties.
description_plain: Your child can decide if line segments, angles, and shapes are the same by using their special properties.

subject: Math
description: Draw lines of symmetry to divide regular polygons into two congruent parts.
description_plain: Your child can draw a line that splits regular shapes into two matching halves.

subject: Math
description: The student will determine congruence of segments, angles, and polygons.
description_plain: Your child will learn how to tell if line segments, angles, and shapes are exactly the same.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices; use coordinates to determine the length of a side joining points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate. Ordered pairs will be limited to coordinates expressed as integers. Apply these techniques in the context of solving contextual and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw shapes on a grid using points with whole number coordinates and find the length of sides when points share the same x or y value, solving real problems.

subject: Math
description: Relate the coordinates of a point to the distance from each axis and relate the coordinates of a single point to another point on the same horizontal or vertical line. Ordered pairs will be limited to coordinates expressed as integers.
description_plain: Your child understands how a point's coordinates show its distance from the axes and can compare points that line up horizontally or vertically.

subject: Math
description: Identify ordered pairs represented by points in the four quadrants and on the axes of the coordinate plane. Ordered pairs will be limited to coordinates expressed as integers.
description_plain: Your child can find and name points on all parts of the coordinate grid using whole number coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Graph ordered pairs in the four quadrants and on the axes of a coordinate plane. Ordered pairs will be limited to coordinates expressed as integers.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a coordinate grid in all four sections and on the lines using whole number coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe the location (quadrant or the axis) of a point given as an ordered pair. Ordered pairs will be limited to coordinates expressed as integers.
description_plain: Your child can say where a point is located on the grid, naming its section or if it’s on an axis, using whole number coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Identify and label the axes, origin, and quadrants of a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can identify and label the parts of a coordinate grid, including the axes, origin, and four sections.

subject: Math
description: The student will describe the characteristics of the coordinate plane and graph ordered pairs.
description_plain: Your child will learn about the coordinate grid and practice plotting points on it.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems, including those in context, involving the perimeter and area of triangles and parallelograms.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the perimeter and area of triangles and parallelograms, including real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Develop the formula for determining the area of parallelograms and triangles using pictorial representations and concrete manipulatives (e.g., two-dimensional diagrams, grid paper).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the formulas for the area of triangles and parallelograms by using pictures and hands-on tools like grid paper.

subject: Math
description: The student will reason mathematically to solve problems, including those in context, that involve the area and perimeter of triangles and parallelograms.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems about the area and perimeter of triangles and parallelograms, including real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems, including those in context, involving circumference and area of a circle when given the length of the diameter or radius.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the circumference and area of circles when given the diameter or radius.

subject: Math
description: Develop the formula for circumference using the relationship between diameter, radius, and pi.
description_plain: Your child understands how to create the formula for a circle’s circumference using diameter, radius, and pi.

subject: Math
description: Develop an approximation for pi (3.14) by gathering data and comparing the circumference to the diameter of various circles, using concrete manipulatives or technological models.
description_plain: Your child can find an approximate value for pi (about 3.14) by measuring and comparing the circumference and diameter of different circles.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and describe the relationship between diameter and circumference
description_plain: Your child explores and explains how the diameter and circumference of a circle are related.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and describe the relationship between radius and circumference
description_plain: Your child explores and explains how the radius and circumference of a circle are related.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and describe the relationship between diameter and radius
description_plain: Your child explores and explains how the diameter and radius of a circle are related.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and describe the relationship between:
description_plain: Your child investigates and describes how the diameter, radius, and circumference of a circle are connected.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe chord, diameter, radius, circumference, and area of a circle.
description_plain: Your child can identify and explain parts of a circle like chord, diameter, radius, circumference, and area.

subject: Math
description: The student will identify the characteristics of circles and solve problems, including those in context, involving circumference and area.
description_plain: Your child understands the features of circles and can solve problems about their circumference and area.

subject: Math
description: Estimate, determine, and justify the solution to one and two-step contextual problems, involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with integers.
description_plain: Your child can estimate, solve, and explain answers to one- and two-step real-world problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Simplify an expression that contains absolute value bars | | and an operation with two integers (e.g., –|5 – 8| or |−12| 8 ) and represent the result on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can simplify math expressions with absolute value and two integers and show the answer on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide two integers.*
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide two whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate/model addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of integers using pictorial representations or concrete manipulatives.*
description_plain: Your child can show how to add, subtract, multiply, and divide whole numbers using pictures or objects.

subject: Math
description: The student will estimate, demonstrate, solve, and justify solutions to problems using operations with integers, including those in context.
description_plain: Your child can estimate, solve, and explain answers to problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Estimate, determine, and justify the solution to single-step and multistep problems in context that involve multiplication and division with fractions (proper or improper) and mixed numbers that include denominators of 12 or less. Answers are expressed in simplest form.
description_plain: Your child can estimate, solve, and explain answers to one- and multi-step problems using multiplication and division with fractions and mixed numbers with denominators up to 12, giving answers in simplest form.

subject: Math
description: Estimate, determine, and justify the solution to single-step and multistep problems in context that involve addition and subtraction with fractions (proper or improper) and mixed numbers, with and without regrouping, that include like and unlike denominators of 12 or less. Answers are expressed in simplest form.
description_plain: Your child can estimate, solve, and explain answers to one- and multi-step problems using addition and subtraction with fractions and mixed numbers with denominators up to 12, including regrouping, giving answers in simplest form.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and explain the effect of multiplying or dividing a fraction, whole number, or mixed number by a number between zero and one.*
description_plain: Your child can explore and explain what happens when you multiply or divide a fraction, whole number, or mixed number by a number between zero and one.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide fractions (proper or improper) and mixed numbers that include denominators of 12 or less. Answers are expressed in simplest form.*
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions and mixed numbers with denominators up to 12 and write the answers in simplest form.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate/model multiplication and division of fractions (proper or improper) and mixed numbers using multiple representations.*
description_plain: Your child can show how to multiply and divide fractions and mixed numbers using different methods like pictures or objects.

subject: Math
description: The student will estimate, demonstrate, solve, and justify solutions to problems using operations with fractions and mixed numbers, including those in context.
description_plain: Your child can estimate, solve, and explain answers to problems using fractions and mixed numbers, including real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent powers of 10 with whole number exponents by examining patterns in place value.
description_plain: Your child understands how to show powers of 10 using whole number exponents by looking at place value patterns.

subject: Math
description: Justify if a number between 0 and 400 is a perfect square through modeling or mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can explain if a number between 0 and 400 is a perfect square by using models or logical thinking.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent patterns of perfect squares not to exceed 202 , by using concrete and pictorial models.
description_plain: Your child can find and show patterns of perfect squares up to 202 using objects or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent patterns with bases and exponents that are whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and show patterns with whole number bases and exponents.

subject: Math
description: The student will recognize and represent patterns with whole number exponents and perfect squares.
description_plain: Your child understands and can show patterns involving whole number exponents and perfect squares.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe the absolute value of an integer as the distance from zero on the number line.
description_plain: Your child understands absolute value as how far a number is from zero on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Compare integers, using mathematical symbols (<, >, =).
description_plain: Your child can compare integers using symbols like <, >, and =.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order integers using a number line.
description_plain: Your child can compare and put integers in order using a number line.

subject: Math
description: Represent integers (e.g., number lines, concrete materials, pictorial models), including models derived from contextual situations, and identify an integer represented by a point on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can show integers using number lines, objects, or pictures and can find which integer a point on a number line represents.

subject: Math
description: The student will reason and use multiple strategies to represent, compare, and order integers.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to show, compare, and order whole number integers.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple strategies (e.g., benchmarks, number line, equivalency) to compare and order no more than four positive rational numbers expressed as fractions (proper or improper), mixed numbers, decimals, and percents (decimals through thousandths, fractions with denominators of 12 or less or factors of 100) with and without models. Justify solutions orally, in writing or with a model. Ordering may be in ascending or descending order.*
description_plain: Your child can compare and order up to four positive numbers written as fractions, mixed numbers, decimals, or percents, and explain their answers using models or words.

subject: Math
description: Represent and determine equivalencies among decimals, percents, fractions (proper or improper), and mixed numbers that have denominators that are 12 or less or factors of 100 incorporating the use of number lines, and concrete and pictorial models.*
description_plain: Your child can show and find equal values among decimals, percents, and fractions with denominators up to 12 or factors of 100 using number lines and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Represent and determine equivalencies among fractions (proper or improper) and mixed numbers that have denominators that are 12 or less or factors of 100 and percents incorporating the use of number lines, and concrete and pictorial models.*
description_plain: Your child can show and find equal values among fractions, mixed numbers with denominators up to 12 or factors of 100, and percents using number lines and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Represent and determine equivalencies among decimals (through the thousandths place) and percents incorporating the use of number lines, and concrete and pictorial models.*
description_plain: Your child can show and find equal values between decimals (up to thousandths) and percents using number lines and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and determine the percent represented by a given model (e.g., number line, picture, verbal description), including percents greater than 100% and less than 1%.*
description_plain: Your child can estimate and find the percent shown by a model, including percents less than 1% and more than 100%.

subject: Math
description: The student will reason and use multiple strategies to express equivalency, compare, and order numbers written as fractions, mixed numbers, decimals, and percents.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to show, compare, and order numbers written as fractions, mixed numbers, decimals, and percents.

subject: Math
description: Describe the differences and similarities between solving linear inequalities in one variable and linear equations in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can explain how solving inequalities with one variable is similar to and different from solving equations with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Identify a numerical value(s) that is part of the solution set of as given one- or two-step linear inequality in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can find numbers that solve one- or two-step inequalities with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in context that require the solution of a one- or two-step inequality.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world problems that need solving one- or two-step inequalities.

subject: Math
description: Create a verbal situation in context given a one or two-step linear inequality in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can make up a real-life story that matches a one- or two-step inequality problem with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Write one- or two-step linear inequalities in one variable to represent a verbal situation, including those in context.
description_plain: Your child can write one- or two-step inequalities with one variable to describe a real-life situation.

subject: Math
description: Represent solutions to one- or two-step linear inequalities in one variable algebraically and graphically using a number line.
description_plain: Your child can show the answers to one- or two-step inequalities with one variable using math and by drawing on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and explain how the solution set of a linear inequality is affected by multiplying or dividing both sides of the inequality statement by a rational number less than zero.
description_plain: Your child understands how multiplying or dividing both sides of an inequality by a negative number changes the solution.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of real numbers and the addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division properties of inequality to solve one- and two-step inequalities in one variable. Coefficients and numeric terms will be rational.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step inequalities with one variable by using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: The student will write and solve one- and two-step linear inequalities in one variable, including problems in context, that require the solution of a one- and two-step linear inequality in one variable.
description_plain: Your child will write and solve one- and two-step inequalities with one variable, including word problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in context that require the solution of a two-step linear equation.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems that need a two-step equation to find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Create a verbal situation in context given a two-step linear equation in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can create a real-life story that fits a two-step equation with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Write a two-step linear equation in one variable to represent a verbal situation, including those in context.
description_plain: Your child can write a two-step equation with one variable to describe a real-life situation.

subject: Math
description: Confirm algebraic solutions to linear equations in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can check if their answer to a one-variable equation is correct.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of real numbers and properties of equality to solve two-step linear equations in one variable. Coefficients and numeric terms will be rational.
description_plain: Your child can solve two-step equations with one variable using math rules and rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve two-step linear equations in one variable using a variety of concrete materials and pictorial representations.
description_plain: Your child can use objects and pictures to show and solve two-step equations with one variable.

subject: Math
description: The student will write and solve two-step linear equations in one variable, including problems in context, that require the solution of a two-step linear equation in one variable.
description_plain: Your child will write and solve two-step equations with one variable, including word problems.

subject: Math
description: Use the order of operations and apply the properties of real numbers to evaluate algebraic expressions for given replacement values of the variables. Exponents are limited to 1, 2, 3, or 4 and bases are limited to positive integers. Expressions should not include braces { } but may include brackets [ ] and absolute value bars | |. Square roots are limited to perfect squares. Limit the number of replacements to no more than three per expression. Replacement values may be positive or negative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use the order of operations and math rules to find the value of expressions when numbers replace letters, including small exponents and square roots of perfect squares.

subject: Math
description: Simplify and generate equivalent algebraic expressions in one variable by applying the order of operations and properties of real numbers. Expressions may require combining like terms to simplify. Expressions will include only linear and numeric terms. Coefficients and numeric terms may be positive or negative rational numbers.*
description_plain: Your child can simplify and create equal algebra expressions with one variable by combining like terms and following math rules.

subject: Math
description: Represent equivalent algebraic expressions in one variable using concrete manipulatives and pictorial representations (e.g., colored chips, algebra tiles).
description_plain: Your child can use objects and drawings to show that two algebra expressions with one variable mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Use the order of operations and apply the properties of real numbers to simplify numerical expressions. Exponents are limited to 1, 2, 3, or 4 and bases are limited to positive integers. Expressions should not include braces { } but may include brackets [ ] and absolute value bars | |. Square roots are limited to perfect squares.*
description_plain: Your child can simplify number expressions by following the order of operations and math rules, including small exponents and square roots of perfect squares.

subject: Math
description: The student will simplify numerical expressions, simplify and generate equivalent algebraic expressions in one variable, and evaluate algebraic expressions for given replacement values of the variables.
description_plain: Your child will simplify number expressions, create equal algebra expressions with one variable, and find values when numbers replace letters.

subject: Math
description: Make connections between and among representations of a proportional relationship between two quantities using problems in context, tables, equations, and graphs. Slope may include positive or negative values (slope will be limited to positive values in a contextual situation).
description_plain: Your child can connect different ways to show proportional relationships, like stories, tables, equations, and graphs, including positive and negative slopes.

subject: Math
description: Graph a line representing a proportional relationship between two quantities given the equation of the line in the form y = mx, where m represents the slope as rate of change. Slope may include positive or negative values.
description_plain: Your child can draw a line on a graph that shows a proportional relationship using an equation like y = mx, where m is the slope or rate of change.

subject: Math
description: Graph a line representing a proportional relationship, between two quantities given an ordered pair on the line and the slope, m, as rate of change. Slope may include positive or negative values.
description_plain: Your child can draw a line on a graph that shows how two things change together, using a point and the rate of change, which can go up or down.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe a line with a slope that is positive, negative, or zero (0), given a graph.
description_plain: Your child can look at a graph and tell if the line goes up, down, or stays flat.

subject: Math
description: Determine the slope, m, as the rate of change in a proportional relationship between two quantities given a table of values, graph, or contextual situation and write an equation in the form y = mx to represent the direct variation relationship. Slope may include positive or negative values (slope will be limited to positive values in a contextual situation).
description_plain: Your child can find how fast one quantity changes compared to another from tables, graphs, or real-life examples, and write an equation like y = mx to show this.

subject: Math
description: The student will investigate and analyze proportional relationships between two quantities using verbal descriptions, tables, equations in y = mx form, and graphs, including problems in context.
description_plain: Your child understands how two things are related proportionally by using words, tables, equations, and graphs, including solving real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data represented in histograms by making observations and drawing conclusions. Determine how histograms reveal patterns in data that cannot be easily seen by looking at the corresponding given data set.
description_plain: Your child can look at histograms to notice patterns and make conclusions that are hard to see just by looking at the raw data.

subject: Math
description: Compare data represented in histograms with the same data represented in other graphs, including but not limited to line plots (dot plots), circle graphs, and stem-and-leaf plots, and justify which graphical representation best represents the data.
description_plain: Your child can compare histograms to other types of graphs and explain which graph shows the data best.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and explain how using different intervals could impact the representation of the data in a histogram.
description_plain: Your child can explore how changing the size of the groups in a histogram affects how the data looks.

subject: Math
description: Organize and represent numerical data using histograms with and without the use of technology
description_plain: Your child can organize numbers and create histograms by hand or using a computer.

subject: Math
description: Determine how sample size and randomness will ensure that the data collected is a sample that is representative of a larger population.
description_plain: Your child understands that collecting enough random data helps make sure the sample represents the whole group fairly.

subject: Math
description: Determine the data needed to answer a formulated question and collect the data (or acquire existing data) using various methods (e.g., observations, measurement, surveys, experiments).
description_plain: Your child can decide what data is needed to answer a question and collect it by watching, measuring, asking, or experimenting.

subject: Math
description: Formulate questions that require the collection or acquisition of data with a focus on histograms.
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions that need data collection, especially questions that can be answered using histograms.

subject: Math
description: The student will apply the data cycle (formulate questions; collect or acquire data; organize and represent data; and analyze data and communicate results) with a focus on histograms.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, gather data, organize it into histograms, and analyze the results to explain what the data shows.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and describe the difference between the probability of an event found through experiment or simulation versus the theoretical probability of that same event.
description_plain: Your child can explore and explain how the chance of something happening from experiments or simulations can be different from the expected chance.

subject: Math
description: Describe changes in the experimental probability as the number of trials increases.
description_plain: Your child understands that the chance found in experiments gets closer to the expected chance when more trials are done.

subject: Math
description: Given the results of a statistical investigation, determine the experimental probability of an event.
description_plain: Your child can find the chance of an event happening by looking at results from an experiment.

subject: Math
description: Determine the theoretical probability of an event.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the expected chance of an event happening based on all possible outcomes.

subject: Math
description: The student will use statistical investigation to determine the probability of an event and investigate and describe the difference between the experimental and theoretical probability.
description_plain: Your child can use experiments to find the chance of events and understand how this compares to the expected chance.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe dilations in context including, but not limited to, scale drawings and graphic design.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain when shapes are made bigger or smaller in real-life examples like drawings or designs.

subject: Math
description: Sketch the image of a dilation of a polygon limited to a scale factor of 1 4 , 1 2 , 2, 3, or 4. The center of the dilation will be the origin.
description_plain: Your child can draw a shape that has been resized by certain amounts, using the origin as the center point.

subject: Math
description: Given a preimage in the coordinate plane, identify the coordinates of the image of a polygon that has been dilated. Scale factors are limited to 1 4 , 1 2 , 2, 3, or 4. The center of the dilation will be the origin.
description_plain: Your child can find the new points of a shape after it has been resized by specific amounts, using the origin as the center.

subject: Math
description: The student will apply dilations of polygons in the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can change the size of shapes on a grid by stretching or shrinking them.

subject: Math
description: Given a diagram, determine an unknown side length in a quadrilateral using properties of quadrilaterals.
description_plain: Your child can find a missing side length in a four-sided shape by using what they know about that shape.

subject: Math
description: Given a diagram, determine an unknown angle measure in a quadrilateral, using properties of quadrilaterals.
description_plain: Your child can find a missing angle in a four-sided shape by using what they know about that shape.

subject: Math
description: Sort and classify quadrilaterals as parallelograms, rectangles, trapezoids, rhombi, and/or squares based on their properties: lines of symmetry
description_plain: Your child can group four-sided shapes like rectangles and squares by looking at their lines of symmetry.

subject: Math
description: Sort and classify quadrilaterals as parallelograms, rectangles, trapezoids, rhombi, and/or squares based on their properties: congruence of angle measures, side, and diagonal lengths
description_plain: Your child can group four-sided shapes by comparing their angles, sides, and diagonals to see which are the same size.

subject: Math
description: Sort and classify quadrilaterals as parallelograms, rectangles, trapezoids, rhombi, and/or squares based on their properties: parallel/perpendicular sides and diagonals
description_plain: Your child can group four-sided shapes by checking if their sides or diagonals are parallel or at right angles.

subject: Math
description: Sort and classify quadrilaterals as parallelograms, rectangles, trapezoids, rhombi, and/or squares based on their properties:
description_plain: Your child can sort and name different four-sided shapes based on their special features.

subject: Math
description: Compare and contrast properties of quadrilaterals to determine lines of symmetry
description_plain: Your child can compare four-sided shapes to find out how many lines of symmetry each has.

subject: Math
description: Compare and contrast properties of quadrilaterals to determine congruence of angle measures, side, and diagonal lengths

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child understands how to find and put together information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and listen to a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-level topics or solve problems using available resources
description_plain: Your child will explore topics by listening to related stories and using resources to learn or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to understand and use different ways to share and get information, like pictures, sounds, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell, create, and dictate stories, rhymes, poems, and events in sequential order using drama, props, and/or pictures indicating first, next and last events in a story
description_plain: Your child can retell or create stories, poems, or events in order using acting, props, or pictures to show what happened first, next, and last.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in a variety of oral language activities, including choral speaking and recitation of short poems, rhymes, songs, and stories with repeated patterns and refrains
description_plain: Your child takes part in speaking activities like group reading and saying short poems, rhymes, songs, and stories with repeated parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly with appropriate pacing, prosody, and voice level
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly with a good speed, expression, and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe personal experiences using complete sentences
description_plain: Your child can talk about their own experiences using full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and sharing ideas in front of others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expressing ideas and needs in complete sentences
description_plain: Your child can say what they think or need using complete sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking questions to seek help, get information, or clarify information for further understanding
description_plain: Your child asks questions to get help, learn more, or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respectfully building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly
description_plain: Your child listens to others' ideas and shares their own clearly and kindly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively and following agreed-upon rules for participating in discussions (e.g., waiting for a turn to speak without unnecessary interruptions and staying on topic)
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and follows rules like waiting their turn to speak and staying on topic during talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) on kindergarten topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child joins in different kinds of group talks about kindergarten topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child learns how to communicate, listen, and work well with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together
description_plain: Your child builds skills to talk and work with others to understand and learn together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonetically spell words containing unknown letter-sound correspondences
description_plain: Your child can spell words by sounding them out, even if they don’t know all the letter sounds yet.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, identify statements and questions and use ending punctuation (e.g., period and question mark)
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between statements and questions and use periods and question marks at the end.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, begin each sentence with a capital letter and capitalize the pronoun I
description_plain: Your child will start sentences with capital letters and always capitalize the word I.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to locate specific actions
description_plain: Your child understands and uses action words to show what someone or something is doing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives to describe specific objects (quantity, size, age, shape, color, or location)
description_plain: Your child uses describing words to talk about how things look, like their size, color, shape, or where they are.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use pronouns to identify individuals and groups (he, she, they, his, hers, their(s)
description_plain: Your child uses words like he, she, they, his, hers, and theirs to talk about people or groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use nouns to identify and name people, places, and things
description_plain: Your child uses naming words to talk about people, places, and things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities
description_plain: Your child can say and make longer complete sentences when practicing together.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate
description_plain: Your child uses correct English when speaking and writing and knows when to use formal or casual language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing or dictating simple sentences that include a subject (noun) and predicate(verb) focused on the topic
description_plain: Your child can say or write simple sentences with a person or thing doing an action about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identifying the audience and purpose of the writing (e.g., letters, stories, “all about” book, etc)
description_plain: Your child understands who will read their writing and why they are writing it, like for letters or stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, use prewriting activities, including drawing and suggestions from peers to generate ideas and plan writing or dictation. This includes:
description_plain: With help, your child uses drawing and ideas from friends to plan what they want to write or say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to write in response to texts heard.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to respond to stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose descriptive writing that identifies a topic and provides one or more relevant details
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to describe a topic and give one or more details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose narrative stories in sequential order (beginning, middle, end)
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to tell a story with a beginning, middle, and end.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child learns to write for different reasons and in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will write in a variety of forms, linked to kindergarten content and texts
description_plain: Your child writes in many forms connected to what they learn and read in kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, use phoneme/grapheme (sound/symbol) correspondences to spell grade-level high-frequency words with accuracy
description_plain: With help, your child spells common kindergarten words correctly by matching sounds to letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode (spell) unknown words using logical invented spelling
description_plain: Your child spells new words by using sounds they hear, even if the spelling isn’t perfect yet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode (spell) one syllable words with short vowels and one syllable words beginning or ending in a digraph (e.g., ch, sh, th, wh)
description_plain: Your child spells short one-syllable words with short vowel sounds and words that start or end with sounds like ch, sh, th, or wh.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply spaces between written words in phrase or sentence level writing.
description_plain: Your child puts spaces between words when writing phrases or sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Accurately print first and last names, beginning each with a capital letter
description_plain: Your child can write their first and last names correctly, starting each with a capital letter.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write left to right and top to bottom
description_plain: Your child writes from left to right and from top to bottom on the page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Accurately and automatically print capital and lowercase letters of the alphabet independently
description_plain: Your child can write both uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet on their own and correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use functional pencil grasp for letter formation
description_plain: Your child holds a pencil properly to form letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will print legibly in manuscript
description_plain: Your child writes letters clearly and neatly by hand.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic
description_plain: With help, your child can find simple ways two texts about the same topic are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the purpose of common text features: table of contents, headings, bolded words, and pictures
description_plain: With help, your child can tell why parts of a book like the table of contents, headings, bold words, and pictures are used.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and key details of a text
description_plain: With help, your child can find the main topic and important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer literal (who, what, when, where) or inferential (why, how) questions about what is read
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer simple questions about who, what, when, where, why, and how from what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands main ideas and details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of informational texts heard.
description_plain: Your child listens to different informational texts and uses what they hear to understand and learn new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, monitor listening comprehension
description_plain: With help, your child checks to make sure they understand what they hear when listening to stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast characters, plot events, or settings between two familiar stories
description_plain: With help, your child can tell how characters, events, or places are alike or different in two stories they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts)
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a story show what is happening in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, generate predictions about story characters and events using the text
description_plain: With help, your child can guess what might happen next in a story by looking at the words.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, orally re-tell a familiar story in sequential order (beginning, middle, end)
description_plain: With help, your child can tell a familiar story out loud in the right order: beginning, middle, and end.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, demonstrate knowledge of story structure by orally identifying characters, setting,important events, and details
description_plain: With help, your child can name the characters, setting, important events, and details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, activate prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge and make connections to what is read
description_plain: With help, your child connects what they already know to the story they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of literary texts heard, to include fantasy, humor, fable/fairy tale, realistic fiction, historical fiction, and folklore/tall tale
description_plain: Your child listens to different kinds of stories and uses what they hear to understand and learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in discussions and speaking activities
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they have learned when talking and joining discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Increase and develop breadth of vocabulary knowledge by listening to high quality, complex text
description_plain: Your child will learn more words by listening to interesting and challenging stories or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the purpose of simple reference materials (e.g., pictures, dictionary, digital dictionary)
description_plain: Your child understands how to use simple tools like pictures or dictionaries to find information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use vocabulary from across content areas
description_plain: Your child can use words they have learned from different subjects when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions about words not understood
description_plain: Your child asks questions about words they do not understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discuss meanings of words from a variety of texts and experiences
description_plain: Your child talks about what words mean from different books and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child learns new words and how to understand them better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on kindergarten content and texts heard
description_plain: Your child will build their vocabulary and word knowledge by listening to stories and lessons for kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Reading Strategies, 3-12) - Introduced in Grade Three
description_plain: This skill will be introduced in Grade Three.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in listening to a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary. Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12)
description_plain: Your child listens to several related stories or texts about a topic to learn new words and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, when responding to text through discussions and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from read-alouds to support claims, conclusions, inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12)
description_plain: With help, your child can talk or write about stories by using details from what they heard.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Text Complexity, 2-12) - Introduced in Grade Two
description_plain: This skill will be introduced in Grade Two.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of letter-sound correspondences to read and reread controlled decodable sentences and texts that include phonic elements and irregular words (Reading Fluency, K-12)
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about letter sounds to read simple sentences and stories with practice.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading and listening to a range of texts. This includes fluently reading decodable sentences and gathering evidence from content-rich texts (through read alouds) on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary
description_plain: Your child will learn and understand stories and information by reading and listening to different texts, including reading simple sentences fluently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use letter-sound correspondences to read grade-level high frequency words, including decodable and irregular words, with automaticity and accuracy
description_plain: Your child can read common kindergarten words quickly and correctly, including tricky and easy words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode (read) and encode (spell) words with short vowels, including words beginning or ending in a digraph (e.g., ch, sh, th, wh)
description_plain: Your child can read and spell words with short vowel sounds, including words that start or end with sounds like ch, sh, th, or wh.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of letter-sound correspondences to blend CV, CVC, VC words
description_plain: Your child can blend letter sounds to read simple words like CV, CVC, and VC patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the letter-sound correspondences for consonant digraphs (e.g., ch, sh, th, wh)
description_plain: Your child knows the sounds made by letter pairs like ch, sh, th, and wh.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge that every word has a vowel sound
description_plain: Your child understands that every word has at least one vowel sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discriminate between long and short sounds with common spellings for the five major vowels with open and closed syllables (e.g., short /e/ as in “pet”, long /e/ as in “he”)
description_plain: Your child can hear and tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in words like 'pet' and 'he'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify common letter-sound correspondences
description_plain: Your child can match letters to the sounds they usually make.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify capital and lowercase letters of the alphabet
description_plain: Your child can recognize both uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonics and Word Analysis: The student will apply phonetic principles to read and spell words
description_plain: Your child can use letter sounds to read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ability to segment and blend one syllable words with two, three, and four phonemes (including words with consonant digraphs th, sh, ch, wh)
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together one-syllable words made of two to four sounds, including sounds like 'th', 'sh', 'ch', and 'wh'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate the initial, medial, and final sounds in three phoneme (individual sound) words (excluding words ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/)
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle, and last sounds in simple three-sound words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment one syllable words by onset and rime.
description_plain: Your child can blend and separate parts of one-syllable words by their beginning and ending sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ability to segment spoken words in sentences and syllables in words
description_plain: Your child can break sentences into words and words into syllables by listening carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonological and Phonemic Awareness: The student will orally identify and produce various phonemes (individual sounds) within words to develop phonemic awareness in support of decoding (reading) and encoding (spelling)
description_plain: Your child can listen to and say individual sounds in words to help with reading and spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation)
description_plain: Your child can recognize what makes a sentence, like the first word, capital letters, and ending punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover and title of a text
description_plain: Your child can find the front cover, back cover, and title of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each
description_plain: Your child can name the author and illustrator of a book and understand what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge that spoken words are represented in print and separated by spaces
description_plain: Your child knows that spoken words are written down and separated by spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of a sentence, word, and letter
description_plain: Your child understands what sentences, words, and letters are.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right and from top to bottom on a printed page, including the return sweep from line to line
description_plain: Your child can follow words on a page from left to right and top to bottom, moving to the next line correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print Concepts: The student will apply knowledge of how print is organized and read
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and how to read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: In small or large group settings, informally share recorded information collected from research.
description_plain: Your child can share information they found during research with others in small or large groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use drawing, writing, or dictation to record facts and information collected from research.
description_plain: Your child can draw, write, or say facts they learned from research to record them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use templates to organize the information collected (e.g., charts, graphs).
description_plain: Your child can use charts or graphs to organize information they collected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate and collect information related to the given topic from pictures, texts, people, or provided sources.
description_plain: Your child can find and gather information about a topic from pictures, books, people, or other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, generate research questions related to a given topic.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about a topic with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child learns how to think about and combine information from what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and listen to a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-level topics or solve problems using available resources.
description_plain: Your child will research and listen to related stories or texts to learn about topics or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child uses different ways like pictures, sounds, and words to understand and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell, create, and dictate stories, rhymes, poems, and events in sequential order using drama, props, and/or pictures indicating first, next, and last events in a story.
description_plain: Your child can retell or create stories, poems, or events in order using acting, props, or pictures to show what happened first, next, and last.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in a variety of oral language activities, including choral speaking and recitation of short poems, rhymes, songs, and stories with repeated patterns and refrains.
description_plain: Your child joins in speaking activities like group reading and saying poems, songs, or stories with repeated parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly with appropriate pacing, prosody, and voice level.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly with the right speed, expression, and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details and using appropriate vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child describes people, places, things, and events using good details and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and sharing ideas with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expressing ideas and needs in complete sentences.
description_plain: Your child can say their ideas and needs using full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking questions to seek help, get information, or clarify information for further understanding.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to get help or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respectfully building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child listens to others’ ideas and shares their own clearly and kindly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively and following agreed-upon rules for participating in discussions (e.g., waiting for a turn to speak without unnecessary interruptions and staying on topic).
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and follows rules during talks, like waiting their turn and staying on topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) on grade one topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks about first grade topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child practices listening, talking, and working with others to learn together.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child learns to speak and work well with others to understand and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling of words with commonly taught spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly using common spelling rules and remembers tricky words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify statements and questions and, use correct ending punctuation (e.g., period, questions mark, and exclamation points).
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between statements and questions and uses the right ending punctuation like periods and question marks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence, proper nouns, and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the first word in a sentence, names, and the word I.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use interrogatives to ask questions in complete sentences (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child asks questions using words like who, what, where, when, why, and how in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use articles correctly (e.g., a, an, the).
description_plain: Your child can use the words 'a,' 'an,' and 'the' correctly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use proper verb tense and correct subject-verb agreement.
description_plain: Your child will use the right verb tense and match verbs correctly with their subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use simple verb tenses (past, present, and future) for regular verbs.
description_plain: Your child can make and use simple past, present, and future verb forms for regular verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives to describe specific objects (quantity, size, age, shape, color, or location).
description_plain: Your child uses common describing words to talk about things like how many, size, age, shape, color, or where something is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal and possessive pronouns to represent nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'I,' 'you,' 'he,' 'she,' and 'my' to replace names of people or things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding ‘s’ or ‘es’ sound.
description_plain: Your child can say plural nouns by adding 's' or 'es' sounds when talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand simple sentences, including a noun, verb, and adjective.
description_plain: Your child can make and make longer simple sentences that include a person or thing, an action, and a describing word.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child understands when to use formal English and when it’s okay to speak more casually in talking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, edit writing for conventions e.g., spelling, capitalization, usage, punctuation). (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: With help, your child can fix spelling, capitalization, punctuation, and word use mistakes in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing a series of simple sentences focused on the topic, including sentences with a subject and predicate, subject/verb agreement, and one or more descriptive adjectives.
description_plain: Your child writes simple sentences about a topic that have a subject and action, match subjects and verbs correctly, and use describing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identifying the audience and purpose of the writing (e.g., letters, stories, journals, etc.).
description_plain: Your child knows who they are writing for and why, like writing a letter, story, or journal.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, use a variety of prewriting activities, such as brainstorming, drawing and graphic organizers, to generate ideas and draft writing or dictation. This includes:
description_plain: With help, your child uses activities like drawing and brainstorming to come up with ideas and start writing or telling their story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing about text(s) read or heard in which students share their thinking with a couple of supporting details from the text.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to share their thoughts about something they read or heard, including a couple of details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces that state an opinion and supply a reason for the opinion.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to say what they think and give a reason for their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/expository texts that name a topic and supply some facts about the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to tell about a topic and give some facts about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to recount two or more sequenced events or experiences and include details about the events and characters.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to tell about two or more events in order and include details about what happened and who was there.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child learns to write in different ways for different reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will write in a variety of forms for diverse audiences and purposes linked to grade one content and texts
description_plain: Your child writes in many forms for different readers and purposes connected to what they are learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme/grapheme (sound/symbol) correspondences to encode (spell) grade-level high-frequency words with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can spell common grade-level words quickly and correctly by matching sounds to letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode (spell) unfamiliar words by recognizing and applying taught word patterns.
description_plain: Your child can spell new words by using word patterns they have learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode (spell) 2-syllable words (e.g., pancake) following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child can spell two-syllable words by breaking them into parts and following simple spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme-grapheme (sound/symbol) correspondence to encode (spell) single-syllable words containing closed syllables (cvc, ccvc, cvcc, and ccvcc), open syllables (cv, ccv), vowel-consonant-e (cvce, ccvce).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words by matching sounds to letters, including different syllable types like closed, open, and vowel-consonant-e.

subject: Language Arts
description: Accurately and automatically apply spaces between written words in phrases or sentencelevel writing.
description_plain: Your child will put spaces correctly between words when writing sentences or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Accurately and automatically form all capital and lowercase letters and numbers using manuscript letter formation.
description_plain: Your child can write all capital and lowercase letters and numbers neatly using manuscript handwriting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use functional pencil grasp for letter formation.
description_plain: Your child uses a proper pencil grip to write letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will print legibly in manuscript
description_plain: Your child will print letters clearly and neatly using manuscript style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find simple similarities and differences between two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between information from pictures and information from the words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and use common text features to gain information: table of contents, headings, bolded words, and pictures, captions, and diagrams.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of a book like the table of contents, headings, bold words, pictures, captions, and diagrams to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the difference between facts and opinions in a text.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between facts (true information) and opinions (what someone thinks) in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main idea and supporting details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea and important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer literal (who, what, when, where) and inferential (why, how) questions about what is read, including demonstrating an understanding of the main topics.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about who, what, when, where, why, and how to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child learns to find important ideas and details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of informational texts heard or read
description_plain: Your child uses information from texts they read or hear to understand and learn new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures or experiences of characters in stories heard, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can compare the adventures or experiences of characters in stories using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between characters, settings, and major events in stories heard, using key details.
description_plain: Your child makes connections between characters, places, and main events in stories using key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Set a purpose for reading by providing a guiding question, activating prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge.
description_plain: Your child sets a goal for reading by asking a question and thinking about what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generate predictions about story characters and events using the text.
description_plain: Your child makes guesses about what characters will do or what will happen next in a story using clues from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer literal (who, what, when, where) or inferential (how, why) questions about what is read.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about who, what, when, where, why, and how to understand stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the elements of a familiar story, including the characters, setting, and important events (e.g., conflict and resolution).
description_plain: Your child can name the characters, where the story happens, and the important events like problems and how they get solved in a story they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell familiar stories sequentially and demonstrate an understanding of the story structure, the central message or lesson, and the details.
description_plain: Your child can retell a story in the right order and explain the main idea or lesson and important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of literary texts heard or read including fantasy, humor, fable/fairy tale, realistic fiction, historical fiction, and folklore/tall tale
