description_plain: Your child understands stories they hear or read, like fairy tales, funny stories, and real-life stories, by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in discussions and speaking activities.
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases when talking and sharing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Increase and develop breadth of vocabulary knowledge by listening to high quality, complex text.
description_plain: Your child learns more words by listening to interesting and challenging stories or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the purpose of simple reference materials (e.g. picture dictionary, digital dictionary).
description_plain: Your child knows how to use simple tools like picture dictionaries or online dictionaries to find word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs and adjectives
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words and describing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of an unknown word using frequently occurring root words and inflectional affixes (e.g. -s, -ing, -ed).
description_plain: Your child figures out what new words mean by looking at parts of the word like endings or beginnings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use vocabulary across content areas.
description_plain: Your child uses new words learned in different subjects and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for the meaning of unknown words and make connections to familiar words.
description_plain: Your child asks about words they don’t know and connects them to words they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify antonyms and synonyms of familiar words to deepen understanding of word meaning and relationships.
description_plain: Your child learns words that mean the opposite or the same to better understand word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discuss meanings of words in context from a variety of texts.
description_plain: Your child talks about what words mean by looking at how they are used in different stories or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child learns new words and how to understand them better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on gradeone content and texts heard or read
description_plain: Your child builds their vocabulary by learning new words from stories and lessons at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Reading Strategies, 3-12): Introduced in Grade Three.
description_plain: This skill will be introduced in Grade Three.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in listening to a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary. Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child listens to several related texts on the same topic to learn more words and ideas and uses this to understand new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, when responding to text through discussions and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from read-alouds to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: With help, your child can find and use details from stories they hear to support their ideas when talking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Text Complexity, 2-12) Introduced in Grade Two.
description_plain: This skill will be introduced in Grade Two.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of letter-sound correspondences to read a variety of decodable and gradelevel texts with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or selfcorrect word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about letters and sounds to read books accurately and smoothly, checking their reading to understand the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading and listening to a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading decodable texts and gathering evidence from reading widely (through content-rich read alouds) on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary
description_plain: Your child learns new information and understands stories by reading and listening to different kinds of texts, including easy-to-read books and interesting read-alouds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write grade-level high-frequency words with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can write common first-grade words quickly and correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level high-frequency words, including decodable and irregular words with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can read common first-grade words, including easy and tricky ones, quickly and correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllabic words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child can read longer words by breaking them into smaller parts called syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use letter-sound correspondences to decode words containing common vowel teams and rcontrolled vowels.
description_plain: Your child can sound out words that have common vowel pairs and special vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words with long vowels, open syllables, (CV, CCV) and vowelconsonant-e (CVCE, CCVCE).
description_plain: Your child can read and spell words with long vowel sounds and special syllable patterns like vowel-consonant-e.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words with short vowels to include blends with digraphs, closed syllables (CVC, CCVC, CVCC, CCVCC).
description_plain: Your child can read and spell words with short vowel sounds and blends of letters in different syllable types.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonics and Word Analysis: The student will apply phonetic principles to read and spell words
description_plain: Your child understands how to use sounds and letters to read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to segment words with four and five phonemes, including words with consonant digraphs (e.g., th, sh, ch) and consonant blends e.g., fr, st, bl).
description_plain: Your child can break apart words with four or five sounds, including sounds made by two letters together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ability to blend words with four and five phonemes, including words with consonant digraphs (e.g., th, sh, ch, wh) and consonant blends (e.g., fr, st, bl).
description_plain: Your child can put together words with four or five sounds, including sounds made by two letters together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate sounds in four and five phoneme words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say individual sounds in words that have four or five sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonological and Phonemic Awareness: The student will orally identify and produce various phonemes (individual sounds) within words to develop phonemic awareness in support of decoding (reading) and encoding (spelling)
description_plain: Your child can listen to and say the different sounds in words to help with reading and spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child can recognize parts of a sentence like the first word, capital letters, and ending punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover and title of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the front cover, back cover, and title of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each.
description_plain: Your child can name the author and illustrator of a book and explain what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge that spoken words are represented in print and separated by spaces.
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words are written down and separated by spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of a sentence, word, and letter.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between a sentence, a word, and a letter.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right and from top to bottom on a printed page, including the return sweep from line to line.
description_plain: Your child can follow words on a page from left to right and top to bottom, moving correctly from one line to the next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print Concepts: The student will apply knowledge of how print is organized and read
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and how to read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Share information orally in writing, or through visual display, avoiding plagiarism and using own words.
description_plain: Your child can share information by speaking, writing, or showing pictures, using their own words and not copying others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Record information on sources using own words, organizing evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child can write down information from different sources in their own words and organize it into groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use templates to organize the information collected (e.g., charts, graphs).
description_plain: Your child can use charts or graphs to organize information they have collected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate information in reference texts, electronic resources, interviews, or provided sources.
description_plain: Your child can find information by looking in books, using computers, asking people, or using other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a topic and generate questions to explore the topic.
description_plain: Your child can pick a topic and come up with questions to learn more about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child can look at information carefully and put it together to understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read or listen to a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-level topics or solve problems using available resources.
description_plain: Your child will do research and read or listen to related texts to learn about topics or solve problems using different resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create a simple presentation using multimodal tools that enhance the topic or presentation.
description_plain: Your child can make a simple presentation using pictures, sounds, or other tools that help explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child can use different ways like pictures, sounds, and words together to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell information in an organized manner, focused on a key topic or experience.
description_plain: Your child can retell information clearly and in order, focusing on the main topic or experience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create and participate in oral language activities that include oral storytelling and dramatics.
description_plain: Your child can take part in speaking activities like telling stories or acting out scenes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage the audience by asking and/or responding to questions.
description_plain: Your child can keep the audience interested by asking questions or answering them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly with appropriate pacing, prosody, and voice level.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly with the right speed, expression, and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use topic specific language and vocabulary to communicate ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use words related to the topic to share their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child can speak and present their ideas clearly to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrating active engagement when listening to a speaker and asking for clarification to ensure understanding.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully to others and asks questions when they don’t understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking and responding to questions that acquire or confirm information on a topic.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to get or check information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respectfully building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child listens to others’ ideas respectfully and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively and following agreed upon rules for participating in discussions.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and follows the rules when joining group talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) on grade two topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of discussions with others about grade two topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child can listen, talk, and work well with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child will learn how to speak and work with others to understand and share ideas together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communications and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child understands different ways to communicate using speaking, writing, and other media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words.
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned when writing new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in salutation and closing of a letter.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use commas correctly in the greeting and closing of a letter.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify statements, questions, and commands and use correct ending punctuation (e.g., period, question mark, and exclamation points).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between statements, questions, and commands and use the right punctuation at the end.

subject: Language Arts
description: Eliminate double negatives when speaking.
description_plain: Your child avoids using double negatives when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use contractions and singular possessives.
description_plain: Your child uses contractions and shows possession with singular nouns correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use subject-verb agreement in simple sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses matching subjects and verbs in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use proper verb tense, including for frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., ran, told, went).
description_plain: Your child uses the correct verb tense, including common irregular verbs like ran, told, and went.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child uses common connecting words like and, but, or, so, and because to show simple relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., men, teeth).
description_plain: Your child can form and use regular and common irregular plural nouns like men and teeth.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use indefinite (e.g., anybody, anything) reflexive (e.g., yourself, herself) pronouns.
description_plain: Your child uses indefinite pronouns like anybody and reflexive pronouns like yourself correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences, both simple and compound.
description_plain: Your child can make complete sentences and expand them into longer sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English rules when speaking and writing and knows when to use formal or informal language.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, edit writing for conventions (e.g., spelling, capitalization, usage, punctuation). (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: With help, your child can check and fix spelling, capitalization, punctuation, and word use in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising for quality of ideas, organization, sentence fluency, and word choice.
description_plain: With help, your child can improve their writing by making ideas clearer, organizing better, and choosing good words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing, selecting, and organizing ideas and details relevant to the topic, purpose, and genre.
description_plain: Your child can choose and organize ideas and details that fit the topic and purpose of their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identifying the audience and purpose of the writing.
description_plain: Your child understands who will read their writing and why they are writing it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Writing a clear topic sentence focusing on the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear main sentence that shows the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in writing as a process to plan writing based on purpose and genre. This includes:
description_plain: Your child plans their writing by thinking about the purpose and type of writing they will do.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in response to text(s) read or heard to share thinking with a couple supporting details from the text.
description_plain: Your child can write about something they read or heard and include a couple of details from the text to explain their thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts that support a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child will write their opinion on a topic or story and give reasons to support their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts that introduce a topic and develop the ideas with facts and examples.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic by giving facts and examples to help others understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives that recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events and include details about the events and characters and use of time-related words to signal the event sequence.
description_plain: Your child will write stories that tell about an event or a short series of events, including details about what happened and who was involved, using words that show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child understands different ways to write for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will write in a variety of forms for diverse audiences and purposes, linked to grade two content and texts.
description_plain: Your child will write in many different ways about what they are learning and reading in second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme/grapheme (sound/symbol) correspondences to encode (spell) grade-level high-frequency words with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can spell common second-grade words quickly and correctly by matching sounds to letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme-grapheme (sound/symbol) correspondence to encode (spell) multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Your child can spell longer words with more than one syllable by matching sounds to letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme-grapheme (sound/symbol correspondence to encode (spell) single-syllable words containing closed syllables (cvc, ccvc, cvcc, and ccvcc), open syllables (cv, ccv), vowel-consonant-e (cvce, ccvce), vowel teams, and r-controlled vowels.
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words using different sound and letter patterns, like closed syllables, open syllables, vowel-consonant-e, vowel teams, and r-controlled vowels.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to sign his/her first and last names.
description_plain: Your child will begin to sign their first and last names.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to write capital and lowercase letters of the alphabet in cursive.
description_plain: Your child will start writing both capital and lowercase letters in cursive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain legible printing and begin to make the transition to cursive.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly in print and will begin learning to write in cursive.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will print legibly in manuscript and cursive while applying grade level word knowledge to spell words correctly.
description_plain: Your child will write neatly in both print and cursive and spell words correctly using what they know about words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the interactions between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text are connected or affect each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find and talk about the most important points in two texts about the same topic and explain how they are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge as context for new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they already know to help understand new information they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main reason an author wrote a text, like what question they want to answer or what they want to explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features (table of contents, headings, pictures, captions, maps, and charts) to preview, set a purpose for reading and gain information.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of a book like the table of contents, headings, pictures, captions, maps, and charts to get ready to read and learn new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate facts from opinions within a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts and opinions in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell key details of texts that demonstrate an understanding of the main topics of texts.
description_plain: Your child can retell important details from a text to show they understand the main topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer literal and inferential questions (who, what, where, when, how, and why) about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer simple and deeper questions about important details in a text, like who, what, where, when, how, and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas and important details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate and build knowledge from a variety of grade level complex informational texts heard or read.
description_plain: Your child uses information from texts they read or hear to learn and explain new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast characters, settings, and plots in two versions of the same story from different cultures (e.g., Cinderella stories).
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences between characters, settings, and plots in two versions of the same story from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize dialogue in text and explain how it can reveal characters’ thoughts and perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can recognize when characters talk in a story and explain what their words show about their thoughts and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Set a purpose for reading by providing guiding questions, activating prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge.
description_plain: Your child sets a goal for reading by asking questions and thinking about what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generate predictions about story characters and events using the text.
description_plain: Your child makes guesses about what characters will do or what will happen next in a story using clues from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe character’s attributes (e.g., traits, motivations, or feelings) and how they respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child describes what a character is like and how they react to big events or problems in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a story’s central conflict using events from the plot as evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find the main problem in a story by looking at events in the plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories sequentially, including their overall structure, essential story events, and the central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child retells stories in order, including important events and the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts heard or read to include fantasy, humor, fable/fairy tale, realistic fiction, historical fiction, and folklore/tall tale, with a focus on fables/fairytales from a variety of cultures.
description_plain: Your child uses details from stories they read or hear to understand and learn from different types of stories, like fairy tales, fables, and realistic fiction from many cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in discussions and speaking activities.
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases when talking and sharing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop breadth of vocabulary knowledge by listening to and reading high quality, complex text.
description_plain: Your child learns many new words by listening to and reading interesting and challenging books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs and adjectives.
description_plain: Your child notices small differences in meaning between similar verbs and adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of morphology (e.g., common grade appropriate suffixes, prefixes), synonyms, and antonyms to determine the meaning of new words.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge about word parts like prefixes and suffixes, and words with opposite or similar meanings, to figure out new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the context of a sentence to apply knowledge of homophones.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to understand words that sound the same but have different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of an unknown word using frequently occurring root words and inflectional affixes (e.g. -s, -ing, - ed).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new words by looking at common word parts like roots and endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use vocabulary across content areas.
description_plain: Your child uses words they learn in different subjects and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discuss meanings of new words or phrases acquired through conversations and literature.
description_plain: Your child talks about the meanings of new words they learn from conversations and reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child learns new words and understands how words work by studying their parts and meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on gradetwo content and texts heard or read.
description_plain: Your child will learn new words and understand their meanings by reading and listening to grade 2 stories and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Reading Strategies, 3-12): Introduced in Grade Three.
description_plain: This skill will be introduced in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in listening to a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary. Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child will listen to several related texts about a topic to learn new words and ideas, using what they know to understand more.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussion and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from read-alouds and grade-level texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child will talk or write about what they read by using details from stories or texts to support their ideas, including quoting or explaining parts of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend texts from a variety of literary forms that exhibit complexity at the lower range of the grades 2-3 band (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child will read and understand different types of stories and texts that are suitable for grades 2 and 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child will read grade-level books accurately and smoothly, paying attention to meaning and fixing mistakes while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading and listening to a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts and reading widely (through read alouds) on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child will build understanding and learn new words by reading and listening to challenging texts, reading fluently, and exploring many topics through read-alouds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level high frequency words, including decodable and irregular words, with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child will quickly and correctly read common grade-level words, including those that follow spelling rules and those that don’t.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of affixes (e.g., suffixes, prefixes) to decode words.
description_plain: Your child will use knowledge of word parts like prefixes and suffixes to help read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of syllabication and syllable types to decode words.
description_plain: Your child will use syllables and different syllable types to help read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words with vowel teams and r-controlled vowels.
description_plain: Your child will read and spell words with vowel teams (two vowels together) and vowels controlled by the letter r.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words with short vowels to include blends, digraphs, and trigraphs in closed syllables (CVCC, CCVC, CVCC, CCVCC, and CCVCCC) and open syllables (CV, CCV).
description_plain: Your child will read and spell words with short vowel sounds, including blends and groups of letters, in different syllable patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonics and Word Analysis: The student will apply phonetic principles to read and spell words.
description_plain: Your child will use sounds and spelling rules to read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to segment words with four and five phonemes, including words with consonant digraphs (e.g., th, sh, ch) and consonant blends (e.g., fr, st, bl).
description_plain: Your child will break apart words with four or five sounds, including those with special letter pairs and blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to blend words with four and five phonemes, including words with consonant digraphs (e.g., th, sh, ch) and consonant blends (e.g., fr, st, bl).
description_plain: Your child will put together sounds to say words with four or five sounds, including those with special letter pairs and blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate sounds in four and five phoneme words.
description_plain: Your child will hear and say individual sounds in words that have four or five sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonological and Phonemic Awareness: The student will orally identify and produce various phonemes (individual sounds) within words to develop phonemic awareness in support of decoding (reading) and encoding (spelling)
description_plain: Your child will practice hearing and making different sounds in words to help with reading and spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child will recognize parts of a sentence, like the first word, capital letters, and ending punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover and title of a text.
description_plain: Your child will identify the front cover, back cover, and title of a book or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each.
description_plain: Your child will know who the author and illustrator are and understand what each person does in making a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge that spoken words are represented in print and separated by spaces.
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words are shown as printed words separated by spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of a sentence, word, and letter.
description_plain: Your child knows what sentences, words, and letters are.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right and from top to bottom on a printed page, including the return sweep from line to line.
description_plain: Your child follows words on a page from left to right and top to bottom, moving correctly from one line to the next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print Concepts: The student will apply knowledge of how print is organized and read
description_plain: Your child understands how print is arranged and how to read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Avoid plagiarism, giving credit to sources of information (title and author when available).
description_plain: Your child learns to avoid copying others’ work and gives credit by naming the title and author when possible.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and share information orally, in writing, or through visual display.
description_plain: Your child organizes information and shares it by speaking, writing, or using pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize evidence into relevant categories, recognizing that some sources may be more reliable than others.
description_plain: Your child sorts information into groups and understands that some sources are more trustworthy than others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate information in reference texts, electronic resources, provided sources, or through interviews and take brief notes on sources.
description_plain: Your child finds information in books, online, or by asking people and takes short notes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a topic and generate questions that explore the topic.
description_plain: Your child picks a topic and asks questions to learn more about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child learns to find, understand, and use information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read or listen to a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-three content and texts, solve problems and support cross-curricular learning.
description_plain: Your child researches topics by reading or listening to related texts to learn new things and solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create a simple presentation using multimodal tools that enhance the topic and/or presentation.
description_plain: Your child makes a simple presentation using different tools like pictures, sounds, or videos to help explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to use different ways like speaking, writing, and technology to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using language (formal or informal) and style as appropriate to audience, topic, and purpose.
description_plain: Your child uses the right words and style depending on who they are talking to and what they are talking about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking audibly with appropriate pacing, prosody, and voice level.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly with good speed, expression, and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using descriptive details and appropriate facts to support themes or central ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses details and facts to support the main idea or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner. This includes:
description_plain: Your child tells about a topic, story, or experience in an organized way when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and sharing ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engaging throughout the collaboration.
description_plain: Your child takes part actively when working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking and responding to questions that acquire or confirm information on a topic and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to learn more and connects their ideas to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respectfully building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can listen to others' ideas respectfully and clearly share their own thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively and speaking using agreed-upon discussion rules.
description_plain: Your child will listen carefully and speak politely following discussion rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) on grade three topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child participates in different types of group talks about third-grade topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child practices talking, listening, and working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child learns to speak and work well with others to understand and talk about what they learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries and other tools to check and fix spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use learned spelling patterns when writing words, including high frequency words and grade level word analysis knowledge.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling rules and patterns when writing words, including common and grade-level words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, names, and places.
description_plain: Your child knows to capitalize holidays, people's names, and places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use apostrophes to form contractions and frequently occurring possessions in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses apostrophes correctly for contractions and showing ownership.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in series, dates, addresses, and in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child uses commas in lists, dates, addresses, and in letter greetings and closings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Eliminate double negatives when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child avoids using double negatives when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use subject-verb agreement in simple sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses the right verb form to match the subject in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses regular and irregular verbs correctly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses words that compare things, like bigger or biggest, when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between complete and incomplete sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between complete and incomplete sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange simple and compound sentences when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can make and change simple and compound sentences when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English rules when speaking and writing and knows when to use formal or informal language.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, edit writing for format and conventions such as capitalization, usage, punctuation, and spelling. (See Language Usage for gradelevel expectations).
description_plain: Your child edits their writing with help to fix capitalization, punctuation, spelling, and grammar.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising for quality of ideas, organization, sentence fluency, and word choice.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing with help by making ideas clearer and sentences smoother.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing a concluding statement.
description_plain: Your child adds a closing sentence to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using transition words to vary sentence structure.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'then,' and 'finally' to make sentences more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Elaborating writing by including supporting details.
description_plain: Your child will add details to their writing to explain their ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing, selecting, and organizing ideas relevant to topic, audience,purpose, and genre.
description_plain: Your child can choose and organize ideas that fit the topic and who they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Writing a clear topic sentence focusing on a main idea.
description_plain: Your child will write a clear sentence that tells the main idea of their paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in writing as a process to compose a well-developed paragraph. This includes:
description_plain: Your child understands that writing takes steps and can write a full paragraph with a clear idea and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in response to text(s) read or heard to share thinking using supporting details from the text.
description_plain: Your child can write about what they read or hear and use examples from the text to explain their thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with facts and reasons.
description_plain: Your child will write their opinion on a topic and give reasons and facts to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic that develops the topic with facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic by giving facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional narratives that organize event sequences that unfold naturally.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or made-up events that happen in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize different forms of writing (narrative, expository, and opinion) have distinctive patterns of organization to support their purpose.
description_plain: Your child understands that different kinds of writing, like stories or opinions, have different ways to organize ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child learns to write for different reasons, like telling a story, giving information, or sharing an opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will compose various works for diverse audiences and purposes, linked to grade three content and texts.
description_plain: Your child will write different types of work for different readers and purposes, using what they learn in third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme/grapheme (sound/symbol) correspondences to encode (spell) grade-level high-frequency words with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can spell common third-grade words quickly and correctly by matching sounds to letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common affixes to encode (spell) words.
description_plain: Your child can spell words by using common beginnings and endings like 'un-' or '-ed.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme-grapheme correspondence to encode (spell) multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Your child can spell longer words by matching sounds to letters for each part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form cursive letters with flow from one letter to the next within names and words.
description_plain: Your child can write cursive letters smoothly, connecting letters in names and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sign his/her first and last name.
description_plain: Your child can write their first and last name by hand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write capital and lowercase letters of the alphabet using correct letter formation in cursive.
description_plain: Your child can write both uppercase and lowercase cursive letters correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will print legibly in manuscript and cursive while applying grade level word knowledge to spell words correctly.
description_plain: Your child can write neatly in print and cursive and spell words correctly for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate comprehension by writing about what is read using the text for support.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand what they read by writing about it and using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find and talk about the important points that are the same or different in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge as context for new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they already know to help understand new information they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author’s purpose for writing, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can tell why the author wrote the text and what the author wants to explain or answer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., sidebars, hyperlink) to locate and gain information efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of the text like sidebars or links to quickly find information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe major structural differences between the organizational patterns of different informational texts (e.g., cause/effect, comparison/contrast, problem/solution, description, sequence, and chronological order) and how they support a reader’s understanding of the text.
description_plain: Your child can describe different ways informational texts are organized and how this helps them understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support specific points in texts.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the reasons and facts an author uses to support ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize texts using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause and effect, referring to historical events, scientific ideas, or steps in technical procedures.
description_plain: Your child can summarize what they read using words about time, order, and cause and effect, especially for history, science, or how-to texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of multi-paragraph texts as well as specific paragraphs within them.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a whole text and in individual paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas and important details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from texts to understand and learn from grade-level informational reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters.
description_plain: Your child can compare the themes, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast details in paired literary and informational nonfiction texts including their treatment of similar themes, topics, and patterns of events.
description_plain: Your child can compare details in stories and nonfiction texts that share similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Set a purpose for reading by looking at the illustrations and activating prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge.
description_plain: Your child sets a goal for reading by looking at pictures and thinking about what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the characteristics of different genres.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different types of stories and books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the narrator of a story and the speaker of a poem.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain how an author uses sensory language (e.g., sights, sounds, smells, and tastes) to impact a reader’s understanding of characters, settings, and plot events.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses descriptions of sights, sounds, smells, and tastes to help readers understand the story better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discuss how an author uses characters and settings to advance the plot.
description_plain: Your child talks about how characters and places help move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe a character’s attributes, including their traits, motivations, or feelings and how they develop throughout the text.
description_plain: Your child describes a character’s traits, feelings, and reasons for acting, and how they change in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central conflict and resolution using events from the plot to summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main problem and how it is solved by using events from the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify thematic topics of stories (e.g. friendship, survival, determination) and the lessons learned.
description_plain: Your child can find the main topics in stories, like friendship or determination, and explain the lessons the story teaches.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include fantasy, humor, fable/fairy tale, realistic fiction, historical fiction, biography, autobiography, and folklore/tall tale, with a focus on folklore and tall tales.
description_plain: Your child will understand stories by finding proof in the text and learn from different types of stories like fairy tales, biographies, and especially folklore and tall tales.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in discussions and speaking activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they have learned when talking and sharing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries, beginning dictionaries and thesauruses, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both in books and online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use strategies to infer word meanings.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs and adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar verbs and adjectives by understanding their slight differences in meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop breadth of vocabulary knowledge by listening to and reading high quality, complex text.
description_plain: Your child learns many new words by listening to and reading interesting and challenging books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of morphology, synonyms, and antonyms to determine the meaning of complex words.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge about word parts, synonyms, and antonyms to understand harder words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the context of a sentence to apply knowledge of homophones.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to understand which meaning of a word that sounds the same (homophone) is being used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of complex words using frequently occurring root words and inflectional affixes (e.g. -s, -ing, -ed).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of hard words by looking at common root words and endings like -s, -ing, or -ed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discuss meanings of complex words and phrases acquired through conversations and literature.
description_plain: Your child talks about the meanings of new and difficult words they learn from conversations and books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop general academic language and content specific vocabulary by listening to, reading, and discussing a variety of texts relevant to a grade three topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child learns important school words and subject-specific vocabulary by listening, reading, and talking about different texts related to third grade topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child builds their vocabulary and learns how to understand words better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on gradethree content and texts read or heard.
description_plain: Your child grows their vocabulary and word knowledge by reading and hearing texts about third grade subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attended to common text organizational structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies, 3-12).
description_plain: Your child uses reading strategies like summarizing and asking questions to understand difficult parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in listening to a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary. Use background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary K-12).
description_plain: Your child listens to several related texts on the same topic to learn more and build vocabulary, using what they already know to help understand new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to texts through discussions and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from read alouds and grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: When talking or writing about texts, your child uses several examples from the stories or books to support their ideas and shows where they found the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the higher range of the grades 2-3 bands (See Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand a variety of harder stories and informational texts that are suitable for grades 2-3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex texts with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts accurately and smoothly, with good expression, and checks their understanding as they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading and listening to a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts and reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child builds knowledge and understanding by reading and listening to challenging texts, reads fluently, finds evidence in texts, learns new vocabulary, and uses strategies when they don’t understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level high-frequency words, including decodable and irregular words, with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately read common words for third grade, including both easy-to-sound-out and tricky words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of affixes (e.g., suffixes, prefixes) to decode and encode words.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of word parts like prefixes and suffixes to read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of syllabication and syllable types to decode and encode words.
description_plain: Your child breaks words into syllables and uses syllable patterns to read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words with vowel teams and r-controlled vowels.
description_plain: Your child reads and spells words that have vowel teams and vowels controlled by the letter 'r'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonics and Word Recognition: The student will apply grade level phonics and word analysis skills to decode (read) unfamiliar words in grade level text.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics skills to read new and unfamiliar words in third-grade reading materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: See Kindergarten through grade two for standards that address Phonological and Phonemic Awareness.
description_plain: Your child builds skills in hearing and working with sounds in words, as taught from kindergarten through second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: See Kindergarten for standards that address Print Concepts.
description_plain: Your child understands basic ideas about how print works, as learned in kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Avoid plagiarism and give proper credit by providing citations whenever using another person’s media, facts, ideas, graphics, music, and direct quotations.
description_plain: Your child learns to avoid copying others’ work and gives credit by citing sources when using their ideas, pictures, music, or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and share information orally, in writing, or through visual display.
description_plain: Your child organizes and shares information by speaking, writing, or using pictures and displays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop notes that include important concept, summaries, and identification of information sources.
description_plain: Your child takes notes that include main ideas, summaries, and where the information came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and synthesize information from the print and digital resources determining the relevance and reliability of the information gathered.
description_plain: Your child gathers information from books and online sources and decides if it is important and trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify search terms to locate information on the topic and gather relevant information from various print and digital sources.
description_plain: Your child picks good keywords to search for information and collects useful facts from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct and formulate questions about a topic.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child learns how to find, understand, and use information from different places.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-four content and texts, solve problems, and support cross-curricular learning.
description_plain: Your child researches and reads several related texts to learn about fourth-grade topics, solve problems, and connect ideas across subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how ideas and topics are depicted (e.g., animation, famous images and words, music and sound, photo-editing) in a variety of media and formats.
description_plain: Your child compares how ideas and topics are shown differently in things like cartoons, famous pictures, music, and edited photos.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between auditory, visual, and written media messages (e.g., videos, podcasts, print advertisements) and their purposes (to explain, to inform, to persuade).
description_plain: Your child tells the difference between messages heard, seen, or read (like videos, podcasts, or ads) and understands why they were made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Strategically use two or more interdependent modes of communication to convey the intended message and enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child uses two or more ways of communicating together to clearly share ideas and support main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select, organize, and create engaging presentations that include multimedia components and visual displays.
description_plain: Your child plans and creates interesting presentations that include pictures, videos, or other media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child combines different types of media and communication to share ideas effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encouraging audience participation through planned interactions (e.g., questioning, discussion, gathered responses, and movement).
description_plain: Your child can get the audience involved by planning ways to ask questions, have discussions, gather answers, or use movement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using language (formal or informal) and style as appropriate to the audience, topic, or purpose.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right words and style to match who they are talking to and what they are talking about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking audibly with appropriate pacing, prosody, and voice level.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly with the right speed, expression, and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using descriptive details and appropriate facts to support themes or central ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses details and facts to explain the main idea or theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can give an organized oral report, tell a story, or share an experience clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and sharing ideas out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engaging throughout the collaboration
description_plain: Your child stays involved and participates fully during group work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using evidence, examples, or details to support opinions and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child uses examples and details to support their opinions and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking and answering specific questions to clarify concepts, share, or follow up on information, make connections, and confirm new understanding(s).
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand better and connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respectfully building on others’ ideas and clearly expressing their own.
description_plain: Your child listens to others respectfully and adds their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively and speaking using agreed-upon discussion rules.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and follows agreed rules when talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade four topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child takes part in long group discussions with different people about grade-level topics and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child practices communication, listening, and working together with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child learns to speak and work well with others to understand and talk about what they are learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses books or other resources to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and grade level word analysis knowledge.
description_plain: Your child spells common and studied words correctly using what they know about word parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use apostrophes to form contractions and to show possession in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses apostrophes correctly to show contractions and possession in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to indicate dialogue in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses commas and quotation marks properly to show when someone is speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in series, dates, addresses, and letters in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in lists, dates, addresses, and letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use standard noun-pronoun agreement when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses nouns and pronouns that agree correctly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use standard subject-verb agreement when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct subject-verb agreement when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal words (e.g., can, may, must) to convey various conditions when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can use words like can, may, and must to show different possibilities or needs when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives to compare and describe noun or noun phrases with specificity when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can use adjectives to describe and compare people, places, or things clearly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating (e.g., and, but), subordinating (e.g., although, because) conjunctions to join words and phrases in a sentence.
description_plain: Your child can join words and phrases in sentences using connecting words like and, but, although, and because.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange simple and compound sentences, including prepositional phrases, when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can create, expand, and rearrange simple and compound sentences, including adding phrases that show location or time, when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child understands when to use formal English and when informal language is okay while speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-and peer-edit the writing for capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, paragraphing, and Standard English (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: Your child can check their own and others' writing for correct capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, paragraphs, and proper English.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising for quality of ideas, organization, sentence fluency, and word choice.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can improve their writing by revising ideas, organization, sentence flow, and word choice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear ending sentence or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using transition words and prepositional phrases to vary sentence structure and link sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use transition words and phrases to make sentences interesting and connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing, selecting, and organizing ideas relevant to the topic, purpose, and genre using precise language and topic-specific words and phrases, descriptive details, and sensory language.
description_plain: Your child can choose and organize ideas that fit the topic and purpose, using clear and descriptive words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing an introduction that includes a clear topic sentence that connects to the central idea.
description_plain: Your child can write an introduction with a clear main sentence that connects to the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in writing as a process to compose well-developed paragraphs. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can write well-developed paragraphs by planning, organizing, and adding details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in response to text(s) read (including summaries, reflections and descriptions) to demonstrate thinking with details, examples, and other evidence from the text, using linking words to connect ideas.
description_plain: Your child can write about what they read by summarizing, reflecting, and giving examples using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write persuasive pieces on topics or texts that express a clear opinion supported by facts, details, and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that clearly state their view and support it with facts and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write expository texts to examine a topic that develops the focus with facts, details, or other information and uses linking words to connect ideas.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic with facts and details, using words that connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional narratives that are logically organized around a central problem or experience.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences that are organized around a main problem or event.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize different forms of writing (narrative, expository, and persuasive) have distinctive patterns of organization to support their purpose.
description_plain: Your child understands that different types of writing, like stories, reports, and opinions, have different ways to organize ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child learns to write in different ways for different reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will compose various works for diverse audiences and purposes, linked to grade four content and texts.
description_plain: Your child can write different kinds of work for different audiences and purposes, connected to what they learn in fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme/grapheme (sound/symbol) correspondences to decode (read) and encode (spell) grade-level high-frequency words with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can read and spell common grade-level words quickly and correctly by knowing the sounds letters make.

subject: Language Arts
