description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to spell accurately.
description_plain: Your child will spell words correctly by using what they know about letter sounds, syllables, and word parts like roots and endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sign his/her first and last name.
description_plain: Your child can sign their first and last name.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will print legibly in manuscript and cursive while applying grade level word knowledge to spell words correctly.
description_plain: Your child will write neatly in both print and cursive and spell words correctly using grade-level spelling skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationships between a series of historical events, scientific concepts, or steps in technical procedures using words that pertain to comparison, sequence, or cause and effect.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events, science ideas, or steps in instructions are connected by showing what happens first, next, or why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast multiple accounts of the same event or topic and describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare different stories or reports about the same event and explain how they focus on different details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge as context for new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they already know to help understand new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the author’s purpose for writing, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe based on the connections between particular sentences and paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child can explain why an author wrote a text by looking at how sentences and paragraphs connect to answer questions or explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of text features and search tools in multiple print and digital sources to locate and categorize information efficiently and gain meaning.
description_plain: Your child can use features like headings and search tools in books and online to find and organize information quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how authors select an organizational pattern (e.g., cause/effect, comparison/contrast, problem/solution) using transitional words and phrases to support their purpose and a reader’s understanding of the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors organize their writing using words that show cause and effect, comparisons, or problems and solutions to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between fact and opinion and explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support opinions within texts.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts and opinions and explain how authors use reasons and evidence to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in historical, scientific, or technical texts, including what happened and why.
description_plain: Your child can summarize what happened and why in history, science, or how-to texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the main idea of multi-paragraph texts and the specific paragraphs within them, explaining how key details support the main ideas.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea of a text and explain how important details support that idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands key ideas and details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child uses information from texts to understand and learn from grade-level nonfiction reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the overall structure of stories, poems, and plays and how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can explain how stories, poems, and plays are organized and how each part connects to what came before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast details in paired literary and informational nonfiction texts including their treatment of similar themes, topics, and patterns of events.
description_plain: Your child can compare details in stories and nonfiction texts that share similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Set a purpose for reading by activating prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge.
description_plain: Your child sets a purpose for reading by thinking about what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between first-and third-person point of view.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a story is told by a character in the story (first person) or by someone outside the story (third person).

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the narrator of a story and the speaker of a poem.
description_plain: Your child can identify who is telling a story or who is speaking in a poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the characteristics of different genres of literary texts (e.g., drama, poems, stories) and refer to the structural elements of each.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different types of stories like plays, poems, and stories, and explain how each is organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how an author uses language (dialogue, sensory language, and dialect), characters, and settings to advance the plot.
description_plain: Your child will notice how an author uses conversations, descriptions, and characters to move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze characters in-depth, drawing on specific details from the text, including their words, actions, or a character’s thoughts.
description_plain: Your child can understand characters deeply by looking at what they say, do, and think in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the central conflict and explain the resolution using an understanding of text structure and events from the plot as evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main problem in a story and how it gets solved by using details from the plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the theme of stories, dramas, or poetry, including the thematic topic (e.g., courage, loyalty, family) and how characters respond to challenges.
description_plain: Your child understands the main message or lesson in stories, plays, or poems and how characters deal with challenges.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include fantasy, humor, poetry, realistic fiction, historical fiction, biography, autobiography, and folklore/tall tales, with a focus on poetry, realistic fiction and historical fiction.
description_plain: Your child will use details from different kinds of stories and poems to understand and learn from them, focusing especially on poetry, realistic stories, and history-based stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in discussions and speaking activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they have learned when talking and sharing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries,beginning dictionaries, and thesauruses, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both in books and online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use strategies to infer word meanings.
description_plain: Your child uses clues to figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs and adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar verbs and adjectives by their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop breadth of vocabulary knowledge by listening to and reading high quality, complex text.
description_plain: Your child learns many new words by listening to and reading interesting and challenging books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of morphology, synonyms, and antonyms to determine the meaning of complex words.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge about word parts and similar or opposite words to understand harder words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the context of a sentence to apply knowledge of homophones.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to understand words that sound the same but have different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of complex words using frequently occurring root words and inflectional affixes (e.g. -s, -ing, -ed).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of hard words by knowing common word parts like endings and roots.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discuss meanings of complex words and phrases acquired through conversations and literature.
description_plain: Your child talks about the meanings of new and difficult words they learn from talking and reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop general academic language and content specific vocabulary by listening to, reading, and discussing a variety of texts relevant to a grade four topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child learns important school words and subject words by listening, reading, and talking about different topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child works on learning new words and understanding how words work.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on gradefour content and texts.
description_plain: Your child builds their vocabulary and word skills by reading and learning about grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to common organizational text structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies 3-12).
description_plain: Your child uses helpful reading strategies like summarizing and asking questions to understand hard parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.). Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads several related books on a topic to learn more and grow their vocabulary, reading some on their own and some with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to texts through discussion and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can find and use several examples from grade-level books to support their ideas when talking or writing, including quoting or explaining where the information came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the lower range of the grades 4-5 band (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different kinds of stories and information books that are a bit challenging for grades 4 and 5.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex texts with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books smoothly and with expression, checking and fixing any mistakes to understand the text better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child will read challenging books to learn new information and words, read on different topics, and use strategies to understand when things get tricky.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level high-frequency words, including decodable and irregular words, with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and correctly read common grade-level words, including tricky and regular ones.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of morphology (suffixes, prefixes, root/base) to decode words.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge about word parts like prefixes, suffixes, and roots to figure out how to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of syllabication and syllable types to decode and encode words.
description_plain: Your child uses syllables and different syllable types to help read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonics and Word Analysis: The student will apply grade level phonics and word analysis skills to decode (read) unfamiliar words in grade level text.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read new or hard words in grade-level books.

subject: Language Arts
description: See Kindergarten through grade two for standards that address Phonological and Phonemic Awareness.
description_plain: Your child has learned about sounds in words from earlier grades (kindergarten to grade 2).

subject: Language Arts
description: See Kindergarten for standards that address Print Concepts.
description_plain: Your child has learned about how print works from kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Avoid plagiarism and give proper credit by providing citations whenever using another person’s media, facts, ideas, graphics, music, and direct quotations.
description_plain: Your child knows to avoid copying others’ work and gives credit by citing when using someone else’s ideas, pictures, music, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and share information orally, in writing, or through visual display.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share information by speaking, writing, or using pictures and charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop notes that include important concepts, summaries, and identification of information sources.
description_plain: Your child takes notes that include main ideas, summaries, and where the information came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and synthesize information from the print and digital resources, evaluating their relevance, reliability, and credibility.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and the internet, deciding if it is useful and trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify search terms to locate information and gather relevant information from various print and digital sources to address the research.
description_plain: Your child knows how to choose good search words and find important information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate questions that help narrow the topic and revise questions as needed based on research.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to focus their research and changes questions as they learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child learns how to find, understand, and use information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-five content and texts, solve problems, and support cross-curricular learning.
description_plain: Your child researches and reads related books on topics to learn new things, solve problems, and connect ideas across subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast techniques used in a variety of media messages (e.g., animation, famous images and words, music and sound, photo-editing).
description_plain: Your child can compare how different media like cartoons, famous pictures, music, and photo edits use different techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the purpose, intended audience, and credibility of information (e.g., auditory, visual, and written media messages) being presented.
description_plain: Your child can tell who a message is for, why it was made, and if the information is trustworthy in things they see, hear, or read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Deconstruct various types of media to identify the characteristics and determine the effectiveness of the intended messages.
description_plain: Your child can look at different types of media and explain what makes their messages clear or effective.

subject: Language Arts
description: Strategically use two or more interdependent modes of communication to convey the intended message and enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can use two or more ways of communicating together to share ideas clearly and make their main points stronger.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select, organize, and create engaging presentations that include multimedia components and visual displays.
description_plain: Your child can choose and organize information to create interesting presentations that include pictures, videos, or other media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child understands how to use different types of media and communication together to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encouraging audience participation through planned interactions (e.g., questioning, discussion, gathering responses, and movement).
description_plain: Your child can plan ways to get the audience involved, like asking questions or encouraging discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrating awareness of and sensitivity to the appropriate use of words (e.g., avoiding stereotypes, multiple meanings of words).
description_plain: Your child knows how to choose words carefully to be respectful and clear, avoiding stereotypes or confusing meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using facial expressions and gestures to support, accentuate, or dramatize the message during oral presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses facial expressions and hand movements to help explain or emphasize their message when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrating appropriate speaking techniques (e.g., adequate volume and clear pronunciation) suitable to the audience, purpose, and situations.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly and loudly enough for the audience and situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using content specific vocabulary, appropriate fact,s and relevant descriptive details to support themes or central ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses subject-specific words, facts, and details to support their main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an opinion in an organized manner. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can give organized oral reports or share opinions clearly on a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and presenting their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Share responsibility for the learning based on assigned roles and/or task expectations.
description_plain: Your child shares responsibility by doing their part in group tasks or roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarizing the main ideas being discussed, using evidence, examples, and details to support opinions and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can sum up main ideas and use examples or details to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking and answering relevant questions to build on others’ ideas, clarify ideas, and acquire or confirm information.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand and build on others’ ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respectfully demonstrating agreement or disagreement with others’ ideas.
description_plain: Your child shows respect when agreeing or disagreeing with others’ ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively and speaking using agreed-upon discussion rules.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and follows discussion rules when talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate in a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade five topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child prepares for and takes part in group discussions on grade-level topics, working well with different people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child practices communication, listening, and working together with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child learns to speak and work well with others to understand and share ideas together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Modal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns how to communicate using different types of media and ways of sharing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other reference books to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, syllable patterns, ending rules) when pronouncing and writing words.
description_plain: Your child can use common spelling rules and patterns to help say and write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a hyphen to divide words at the end of a line in writing.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use a hyphen to split a word at the end of a line when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use colons to separate hours and minutes and to introduce a list.
description_plain: Your child can use colons to show time (like hours and minutes) and to start a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas correctly in compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly when joining two sentences with words like 'and' or 'but'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use standard subject-verb agreement when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses the right matching between subjects and verbs when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense and number in writing.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when verb tenses or numbers change incorrectly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use interjections, prepositional phrases, and coordinating and subordinating conjunctions in writing to join words and phrases in a sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses words like interjections, prepositional phrases, and conjunctions to connect words and phrases in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adverbs to express time, frequency, degree, and level of certainty when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses adverbs to show when, how often, how much, or how sure something happens in speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or combine them to make writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English rules when speaking and writing and knows when to use formal or informal language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-and peer-edit the writing for capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structures, paragraphing, and Standard English (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: Your child can check their own and others’ writing for correct capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, and paragraphing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising for quality of ideas, organization, sentence fluency, and precise word choice.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can improve their writing by fixing ideas, organization, sentence flow, and word choice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear ending sentence or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using transition words and prepositional phrases for sentence variety and link one sentence and paragraph to another.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases that help connect sentences and paragraphs smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing, selecting, and organizing ideas relevant to topic, purpose, and genre, using precise and descriptive language and tone-specific vocabulary to enhance the central idea, tone, and voice.
description_plain: Your child picks and organizes ideas that fit the topic and purpose, using clear and descriptive words to make their writing interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introducing a clear topic sentence and logically organizing ideas and factual evidence to support the position in persuasive writing.
description_plain: Your child starts writing with a clear main idea and arranges facts and reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in writing as a process to compose well-developed paragraphs. This includes:
description_plain: Your child practices writing by creating well-organized paragraphs with clear ideas and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in response to texts read (including summaries, reflections, and descriptions) in which students demonstrate their thinking with details, examples, and other evidence from the text that are logically grouped.
description_plain: Your child writes about what they read by summarizing, reflecting, or describing, using details and examples from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write persuasive pieces on topics or texts, including media messages, supporting a clear perspective with adequate facts, reasons, and logically grouped information.
description_plain: Your child writes to persuade others by sharing a clear opinion and supporting it with facts and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write expository texts to examine a topic and convey ideas that develop the focus with relevant facts, concrete details, and examples from multiple sources and are grouped logically.
description_plain: Your child can write clear reports that explain a topic using facts, details, and examples from different sources, organizing the information in a logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional narratives in prose or poetic form that organize the writing around a central problem, conflict, or experience using descriptions or dialogue to develop the experience(s).
description_plain: Your child can write stories or personal experiences that focus on a main problem or event, using descriptions and dialogue to make the story interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child understands different reasons for writing and can use various styles to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will compose various works for diverse audiences and purposes, linked to grade five content and texts.
description_plain: Your child will write different types of pieces for different readers and purposes, using what they learn in fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phoneme-grapheme (sound/symbol) correspondences to decode (read) and encode (spell) grade-level high-frequency words with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can read and spell common fifth-grade words quickly and correctly by knowing the sounds and letters that go together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to spell accurately.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word parts like roots and endings to spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will print legibly in manuscript and cursive while applying grade level word knowledge to spell words correctly.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly in print and cursive and spells words correctly using grade-level word knowledge.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical texts, including what happened and why based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two or more people, events, or ideas are connected or affect each other in history, science, or technical texts, including what happened and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can look at different accounts of the same event or topic and notice important similarities and differences in how they are told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge as context for new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they already know from experience and learning to understand new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the author’s purpose(s) and describe how the author’s perspective (e.g., beliefs, assumptions, biases) influences the meaning of the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and how the author's beliefs or opinions affect what the text means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine text features and search tools in multiple print and digital sources to evaluate and gain meaning from the information found.
description_plain: Your child uses features like headings, indexes, and search tools in books and online to find and understand information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall organization patterns of texts (e.g., cause/effect, comparison/contrast, problem/solution, description, sequence, and chronological) and how each successive part builds on earlier sections, using available transitional words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by cause and effect or problem and solution, and how each part connects to the next using transition words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how an author uses reasons, evidence, and opinions to support points in a text, by identifying (and accurately quoting) which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons, facts, and opinions to support ideas by pointing out which evidence goes with which idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in historical, scientific, or technical texts, including what happened, how, and why.
description_plain: Your child can summarize events, steps, ideas, or concepts in history, science, or technical texts, explaining what happened, how, and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the main ideas of texts and specific paragraphs within them, including how they are developed through the details.
description_plain: Your child can summarize the main ideas of a text or paragraph and explain how details help develop those ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands key ideas and details in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from grade-level informational texts to understand and learn new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast details in paired literary and informational nonfiction texts including their treatment of similar themes, topics, and patterns of events.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast details in pairs of stories and nonfiction texts that share similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first-and third-person narratives.
description_plain: Your child can compare different points of view in stories, including the difference between stories told by a character (first person) and by someone outside the story (third person).

subject: Language Arts
description: Set a purpose for reading by activating prior (experience) and background (content) knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can get ready to read by thinking about what they already know about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the characteristics of a poem and the author’s use of patterns of sound (e.g., rhyme, rhythm, alliteration, consonance) impact meaning.
description_plain: Your child understands how the sounds in a poem, like rhyme and rhythm, help show its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author’s use of language (e.g., synonyms, figurative language, sensory words, dialogue, dialect) and their impact on understanding characters, setting, and plot events.
description_plain: Your child notices how the author's word choices help us understand the characters, setting, and story events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how an author develops a character through what characters say, think, do, and how other characters respond.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows what a character is like through their words, thoughts, actions, and how others react to them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how events from the plot cause the character(s) to change or evolve and how the development of character(s) or settings impact the plot.
description_plain: Your child explains how story events make characters change and how characters or settings affect the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe plots in stories as a sequence of events that develops the central conflict and resolution, including initiating events, climax, and resolution.
description_plain: Your child can tell the order of events in a story, including the problem, the most exciting part, and how it ends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the story or play, including the overarching theme and lessons learned, and explain how they are developed or conveyed through specific details.
description_plain: Your child can retell the story or play, including the main message or lesson, and explain how details show this.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include poetry, fantasy, humor, mystery, adventure, realistic fiction, historical fiction, and folklore/tall tales, with a focus on fantasy.
description_plain: Your child uses details from different kinds of stories, like poems and adventures, to understand and learn from them, especially fantasy stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in discussions and speaking activities.
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases when talking and sharing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries, beginning dictionaries, and thesauruses, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both in books and online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use strategies to infer word meanings.
description_plain: Your child uses clues to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs and adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can tell the small differences in meaning between similar verbs and adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop breadth of vocabulary knowledge by listening to and reading high quality, complex text.
description_plain: Your child learns many new words by listening to and reading challenging stories and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the morphological relationships between words, including how Greek and Latin affixes and roots impact the meaning.
description_plain: Your child learns how parts of words, like roots and prefixes from Greek and Latin, help explain word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of grade-level appropriate synonyms and antonyms to better understand each word.
description_plain: Your child uses words that mean the same or the opposite to better understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the context of a sentence to apply knowledge of homophones.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to understand words that sound the same but have different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of complex words using frequently occurring root words and inflectional affixes (e.g., -s, -ing,-ed).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of hard words by looking at common word parts like endings and roots.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discuss meanings of complex words and phrases acquired through conversations and literature.
description_plain: Your child talks about the meanings of new and hard words they learn from talking and reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop general academic language and content specific vocabulary by listening to, reading, and discussing a variety of grade-five texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new school and subject words by listening, reading, and talking about different topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child builds their vocabulary and learns how to understand and use new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on gradefive content and texts.
description_plain: Your child can learn new words and understand their meanings by reading fifth-grade level books and materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to common organizational text structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies 3-12).
description_plain: Your child can use helpful reading tricks like summarizing and asking questions to understand hard parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.). Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary K-12).
description_plain: Your child will read several related texts on the same topic to learn more and build vocabulary, reading some on their own and some with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussions and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences from texts, including quoting or paraphrasing accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can find and use several pieces of evidence from fifth-grade texts to support their ideas when talking or writing about what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the higher range of the grades 4-5 band (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different kinds of stories and information that are challenging for grades 4 and 5.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex texts with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts smoothly and clearly, checking their understanding and fixing mistakes as they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child will build knowledge and understanding by reading challenging texts, reading a lot about topics, and using strategies when they don’t understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level high-frequency words, including decodable and irregular words, with automaticity and accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and correctly read common fifth-grade words, including tricky and regular ones.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of morphology (suffixes, prefixes, root/base) to decode words.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of words like prefixes and suffixes to help read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of syllabication and syllable types to decode and encode words.
description_plain: Your child can break words into syllables and use syllable types to read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Phonics and Word Analysis: The student will apply grade level phonics and word analysis skills to decode (read) unfamiliar words in grade level text.
description_plain: Your child will use phonics and word skills to read new and unfamiliar words in fifth-grade texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: See Kindergarten through grade two for standards that address Phonological and Phonemic Awareness.
description_plain: Phonological and phonemic awareness skills are taught in earlier grades (Kindergarten to grade 2).

subject: Language Arts
description: See Kindergarten for standards that address Print Concepts.
description_plain: Print concepts are taught in Kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ethical and responsible use of all sources, including the Internet, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and new technologies as they develop.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use information from all sources, including the internet and AI, in an honest and responsible way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Give credit for information quoted or paraphrased using standard citations (e.g., author, article title, webpage, and publication date).
description_plain: Your child can give credit to authors by naming where information or quotes come from, like the author’s name and the article title.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and share findings in formal and informal oral or written formats.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share what they learn by speaking or writing clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop notes that include important concepts and summaries, including quoting, summarizing, and paraphrasing research findings, avoiding plagiarism by using own words and following ethical and legal guidelines for gathering and using information.
description_plain: Your child can take notes that include main ideas and summaries, using their own words and quoting correctly to avoid copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and synthesize information from multiple sources (primary, secondary, digital, and print) evaluating the relevance, usefulness, validity, and credibility of each source.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different sources and decide which ones are useful and trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collect information from multiple sources, using search terms effectively.
description_plain: Your child can find information from many sources by using good search words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate appropriately narrow questions about a research topic and refocus the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can ask clear, focused questions about a topic and change their questions if needed to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child can look at information carefully and put ideas together to understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-six content and texts, solve problems, and support cross-curricular learning.
description_plain: Your child will research and read related texts on topics to learn new things, solve problems, and connect ideas from different subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the characteristics and analyze the effectiveness of a variety of media messages by considering the results and/or impact on the intended audience.
description_plain: Your child can explain what makes different media messages effective by thinking about how they affect the people who see or hear them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how media messages are intentionally constructed to impact a specific audience.
description_plain: Your child understands that media messages are made on purpose to influence certain groups of people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media formats and explain how it contributes to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can understand information from different types of media and explain how it helps explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Craft and publish audience-specific media messages that present claims and findings in a logical sequence.
description_plain: Your child can create and share media messages that are designed for a specific audience and present ideas in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use media and visual literacy skills to select, organize, and create multimodal content that articulates the purpose of the presentation, using two or more communication modes to make meaning (e.g., still or moving images, gestures, spoken language, and written language).
description_plain: Your child uses pictures, words, sounds, and movements together to share ideas clearly and for a purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to combine different ways of sharing information, like images and words, to communicate better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Referencing source material as appropriate during the presentation.
description_plain: Your child knows how to mention where information comes from when giving a presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encouraging audience participation through planned interactions (e.g., questioning, discussion, gathering responses, and movement).
description_plain: Your child encourages others to join in during presentations by asking questions or starting discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using nonverbal skills, such as proper posture and stance, gestures, and eye movement to enhance the overall message.
description_plain: Your child uses body language like good posture, gestures, and eye contact to make their message stronger.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using verbal communication skills, such as volume, tone, and enunciation to enhance the overall message.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly using the right volume, tone, and pronunciation to make their message better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using language, vocabulary, and style appropriate to the audience, topic, and purpose.
description_plain: Your child uses words and style that fit the audience, topic, and reason for speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing evidence to support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child gives facts or examples to support the main point they are making.

subject: Language Arts
description: Clearly communicating information in an organized or succinct manner.
description_plain: Your child shares information clearly and in an organized way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or present an opinion. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can speak about a topic or share their opinion clearly, using skills like organizing ideas and supporting them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and presenting ideas to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluating the effectiveness of participant interactions and one’s own contributions to the collaborative work.
description_plain: Your child thinks about how well they and others work together and how they contribute to group work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrasing and summarizing key ideas being discussed by using ample evidence, examples, or details to support opinions and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can restate and summarize important ideas in a discussion using examples and details to support their thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicating agreement or tactful disagreement with others’ ideas, using carefully constructed responses.
description_plain: Your child can agree or disagree politely with others by giving thoughtful responses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking relevant questions to clarify others’ perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that help understand other people's points of view better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Working respectfully by building on others’ ideas and showing value for others’ ideas and contributions.
description_plain: Your child works well with others by respecting and building on their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively through verbal and nonverbal communication and using agreed-upon discussion rules.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and follows agreed rules during group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Facilitate and contribute to a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade six topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child takes part in and helps lead group talks about topics and books for sixth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child practices skills in talking, listening, and working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child learns to speak and work well with others to understand and think about what they learn together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or other tools to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and consistently spell frequently used words accurately.
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly and consistently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use and punctuate dialogue and direct quotations appropriately in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses quotation marks and punctuation correctly when writing dialogue or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct complete sentences with appropriate punctuation, avoiding comma splices and run-ons in writing.
description_plain: Your child writes complete sentences with correct punctuation and avoids run-on sentences or comma mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use often confusing verbs (e.g., lie/lay, sit/set, rise/raise) correctly in sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses tricky verbs like lie/lay and rise/raise correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phrases and clauses within a sentence and apply appropriate subject-verb agreement when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses phrases and clauses properly and matches subjects with verbs correctly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adverbs to modify verbs, adjectives, and other adverbs to express manner, place, time, frequency, degree, and level of certainty precisely when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses adverbs to clearly describe how, when, where, how often, or how much something happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use pronoun-antecedent agreement, including indefinite and reflexive pronouns when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns correctly, making sure they agree with the words they replace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct simple, compound, and complex sentences to communicate ideas clearly and add variety to writing.
description_plain: Your child writes simple, compound, and complex sentences to share ideas clearly and make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar when speaking and writing and knows when to use formal or informal language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-and peer-edit for capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, paragraphing, and Standard English (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: Your child checks their own and others’ writing for correct capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, and paragraphing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing for clarity of content, word choice, sentence variety, and transition among paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by making ideas clearer, choosing better words, varying sentences, and connecting paragraphs smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear ending or conclusion to their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expanding and embedding ideas to create sentence variety.
description_plain: Your child adds details and varies sentences to make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Selecting vocabulary and information to enhance the central idea, tone, and voice.
description_plain: Your child can choose words and information that make their main idea, tone, and voice stronger and clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using transitions to show relationships between ideas, signal a shift or change in the writer’s thoughts, and make sentences clearer.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to connect ideas, show changes in thoughts, and make sentences easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Elaborating and supporting ideas, using relevant facts, definitions, details, quotations, and/or examples.
description_plain: Your child adds details like facts, definitions, examples, or quotes to explain and support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establishing a central idea incorporating evidence and maintaining an organized structure to fit the form and topic.
description_plain: Your child creates a main idea with evidence and organizes their writing to fit the topic and type of text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing a thesis statement that focuses the topic and introduces the piece clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear thesis statement that focuses the topic and introduces their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generate and organize ideas using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing) to develop multi-paragraph texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child plans, writes, revises, and edits multi-paragraph texts by organizing and developing their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write reflectively in response to text(s) read to demonstrate thinking with details, examples, and other evidence from the text(s).
description_plain: Your child writes about what they read, using details and examples from the text to show their thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write persuasively about topics or texts, including media messages, supporting welldefined claims with clear reasons and evidence that are logically grouped.
description_plain: Your child writes to persuade others by giving clear reasons and evidence that are organized well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write expository texts to examine a topic or concept, logically conveying ideas and information using text structures such as description, comparison, or cause-effect to create cohesion.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic clearly using descriptions, comparisons, or cause-and-effect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to entertain, to share a personal experience, or to alter an existing story that uses narrative techniques to develop the characters, event(s), and experience(s).
description_plain: Your child writes stories to entertain or share experiences, using details to develop characters and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child learns to write in different ways for different reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will compose various works for diverse audiences and purposes, linked to grade six content and texts.
description_plain: Your child writes different kinds of texts for different audiences and purposes connected to sixth-grade topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author’s presentation of ideas or events with another’s, identifying where the texts agree or disagree.
description_plain: Your child compares how two authors present ideas or events and finds where they agree or disagree.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe ideas within and between selections including how specific sentences, paragraphs, or sections contribute to the development of ideas.
description_plain: Your child explains how sentences and paragraphs help develop ideas within and between texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author establishes and conveys a perspective or purpose in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child explains how an author shows their point of view or purpose in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author’s word choice, organizational pattern, and language structure convey the author’s purpose.
description_plain: Your child explains how an author’s word choices and text organization help show the author’s purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the purpose of text features (e.g., boldface and italics type; type set in color; underlining; graphics and photographs; and headings and subheadings).
description_plain: Your child understands why text features like bold words, pictures, and headings are used in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace the argument and specific claims in texts, distinguishing claims that are supported by evidence and reasons, from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child follows an argument in a text and tells which claims have good evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a key individual, event or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in historical, scientific, or technical texts (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).
description_plain: Your child describes how important people, events, or ideas are introduced and explained in history, science, or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize texts, including their main idea(s) and how they are developed with specific details.
description_plain: Your child summarizes a text by telling the main ideas and the details that support them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas and important details in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence from grade-level nonfiction texts to understand and learn new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast details in two or more paired literary fiction and nonfiction texts on the same topic or with similar themes, including how chapters, scenes, or stanzas work together to provide the overall structure of each text.
description_plain: Your child can compare details in two or more stories or nonfiction texts about the same topic and explain how parts like chapters or scenes fit together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how the interactions between individuals, settings, events, and ideas within a text influence one another.
description_plain: Your child understands how people, places, events, and ideas in a story affect each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view (e.g., first-person, third person limited, third-person omniscient) of the narrator or speaker in a text and influences how events are described in stories, plays, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the narrator’s point of view (like first-person or third-person) shapes how the story is told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain elements of author’s style as purposeful choices (e.g., imagery, figurative language, and word choice) to develop tone.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s word choices and use of images or figures of speech create the mood or feeling in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the poetic elements in prose and poetry (e.g., rhyme, rhythm, repetition, alliteration, and onomatopoeia) and their intended impact on the reader.
description_plain: Your child can describe poetic features like rhyme, rhythm, and repetition in poems or stories and explain how they affect the reader.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the role of the protagonist and antagonist on plot events.
description_plain: Your child understands how the main character and the opposing character influence what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how static and dynamic characters impact the plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how characters who change or stay the same affect the story’s events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between internal and external conflicts (e.g., individual vs. Individual, individual vs. Nature, individual vs. Society, individual vs. Technology, and individual vs. Self) and explain how they impact character development and plot.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between problems inside a character’s mind and problems with outside forces, and explain how these problems affect the story and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe plot developments in stories and dramas by examining the exposition, initiating event, central conflict, rising action, climax, falling action, and resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe the parts of a story’s plot, like the beginning, main problem, important events, turning point, and ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize texts, including determining the central theme of stories, plays, or poems, and how they are conveyed through specific details.
description_plain: Your child can summarize stories, plays, or poems by finding the main message and explaining how details show that message.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include short stories, literary nonfiction, novels, poetry, and drama.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence from different kinds of stories, poems, and plays to understand and learn from them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in multiple contexts, including in students’ discussions and speaking and writing activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases in speaking and writing in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general and specialized word-reference materials, print and digital, to identify word origins, derivations, and pronunciations.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources, both print and online, to find where words come from and how to say them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Clarify the meaning of an unknown word or select the applicable definition of a word from a text by using word reference materials.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown words by using word reference tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the construction and meaning of figurative language, including simile, hyperbole, metaphor, and personification.
description_plain: Your child can explain how similes, metaphors, exaggerations, and giving human traits to things add meaning to language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words, including synonyms and antonyms to better understand each word.
description_plain: Your child can use words with similar or opposite meanings to better understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of Greek and Latin roots and affixes to predict the meaning of unfamiliar words.
description_plain: Your child can use knowledge of Greek and Latin word parts to guess the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context and sentence structure to determine multiple meanings of words and clarify the meanings of unfamiliar words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use the words around a new word and sentence clues to figure out what the word means, even if it has more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and accurately use general academic language and content-specific vocabulary by listening to, reading, and discussing a variety of grade-six texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic and subject-specific words by listening, reading, and talking about different sixth-grade topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child will build their vocabulary and learn how to understand and use new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on grade six content and texts.
description_plain: Your child will steadily grow their word knowledge by reading and learning from sixth-grade materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to text structure, common organizational structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies 3-12).
description_plain: Your child can use different reading strategies, like summarizing and asking questions, to understand hard parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary (These texts should be at a range of complexity level so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.). Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child will read several related texts on a topic to learn more and build vocabulary, sometimes reading alone or with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussion and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can find and use several pieces of evidence from sixth-grade texts to support their ideas when talking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the lower range of the grade 6-8 band (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis chart for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different stories and information texts that are suitable for grades 6 to 8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding when necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts smoothly and clearly, checking their understanding and fixing mistakes while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child will improve understanding by reading challenging texts, reading on topics to learn new words, and using strategies when they get stuck.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: See Kindergarten through grade five for the Foundations for Reading standards.
description_plain: Your child will build reading skills based on what they learned from kindergarten through fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ethical and responsible use of all sources, including the Internet, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and new technologies, as they develop.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use all information sources, like the internet and AI, honestly and responsibly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Give credit for information quoted or paraphrased, using standard citations (e.g., author, article title and webpage, and publication date).
description_plain: Your child can give credit to authors when using their words or ideas by listing where the information came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and share findings in formal and informal oral written formats.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share what they learn through speaking or writing in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote, summarize, and paraphrase research findings from primary and secondary sources, avoiding plagiarism by using own words and following ethical and legal guidelines.
description_plain: Your child can quote, summarize, and explain research using their own words and follow rules to avoid copying others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and analyze the relevance, validity, and credibility of each source (primary, secondary, digital, and print), determining what information to include and exclude.
description_plain: Your child can decide if information sources are trustworthy and useful, choosing what to include or leave out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collect, organize, and synthesize information from multiple sources using various notetaking formats.
description_plain: Your child can gather and organize information from many sources using different note-taking methods.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate questions about a research topic, broadening or narrowing the inquiry as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about a research topic and change them to focus better or explore more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child will learn how to find, understand, and combine information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade seven content and texts, solve problems, and support cross-curricular learning.
description_plain: Your child will research and read related texts on topics to learn more, solve problems, and connect ideas across subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the effectiveness of techniques in auditory, visual, and written media messages (e.g., authorship, format, content, purpose) on the intended audience.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different types of media, like audio, pictures, and writing, use techniques to reach their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze media messages for facts, opinions, persuasive messages, word choice, and viewpoint.
description_plain: Your child can recognize facts, opinions, and persuasive messages in media and understand the words and viewpoints used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain persuasive/informative techniques used in media to sway the audience (e.g., innuendo, card stacking, bandwagon, and appeal to emotions).
description_plain: Your child understands how media uses tricks like emotional appeals or popular opinions to persuade people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Craft and publish audience-specific media messages that present claims and findings with relevant evidence in a logical sequence.
description_plain: Your child can create and share media messages for a specific audience that clearly present ideas with supporting evidence in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use medial and visual literacy skills to select, organize, and create multimodal content that articulates and enhances the purpose of the presentation using two or more communication modes to make meaning (e.g., still or moving images, gestures, spoken language, and written language).
description_plain: Your child can choose and combine pictures, gestures, speech, and writing to make presentations that clearly share their message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child understands how to use different types of media together to communicate ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Memorize and recite a poem demonstrating inflection and meaningful expression that is appropriate to the tone and voice of the selection.
description_plain: Your child can memorize and recite a poem using expression and tone that fits the poem’s mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Referencing source material as appropriate during the presentation.
description_plain: Your child knows how to mention sources when giving a presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Responding to audience questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can answer audience questions with clear evidence and thoughtful ideas.

subject: Language Arts
