description: Adjusting verbal and nonverbal communication skills appropriate to audience, topic, and purpose to enhance the overall message.
description_plain: Your child adjusts how they speak and use body language to fit the audience and topic to make their message better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing evidence to support the main ideas, including pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples.
description_plain: Your child supports main ideas with facts, details, and examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Clearly communicating information in an organized and succinct manner.
description_plain: Your child shares information clearly and in an organized way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an opinion. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can give oral reports or share opinions on a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and presenting ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluating the effectiveness of participant interactions and one’s own contributions to small group activities.
description_plain: Your child thinks about how well they and others work together in small groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrasing, summarizing, and writing reflectively in response to the ideas being discussed.
description_plain: Your child can restate, summarize, and write about ideas discussed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicating agreement or tactful disagreement with others’ ideas using carefully constructed statements.
description_plain: Your child can agree or disagree politely using thoughtful statements.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking and responding to probing questions and providing appropriate feedback within structured discussions.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers deep questions and gives helpful feedback during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Working effectively and respectfully by building on others’ ideas, actively contributing relevant and well-supported ideas and opinions, and sharing responsibility for the collaborative work.
description_plain: Your child works well with others by building on ideas, sharing their own, and helping with group tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively through verbal and nonverbal communication and using agreedupon discussion rules.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully using words and body language and follows discussion rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Facilitate and contribute to a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade seven topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child takes part in and helps lead thoughtful group discussions on grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and work well with others to share ideas and understand what they learn together.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child will practice speaking clearly and cooperating with classmates to learn and understand new information as a group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other tools to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and consistently spell frequently used words accurately
description_plain: Your child will spell common words correctly every time they write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use and punctuate dialogue and direct quotations appropriately in writing.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use quotation marks and punctuation correctly when writing dialogue or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct complete sentence with appropriate punctuation, avoiding comma splices and run-ons in writing.
description_plain: Your child can write complete sentences with correct punctuation and avoid mistakes like run-on sentences or comma splices.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistent verb tense across paragraphs in writing.
description_plain: Your child keeps the same verb tense throughout their writing so it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Arrange phrases and clauses within a sentence and apply appropriate subject-verb agreement to improve meaning, reader/listener interest, and style in writing.
description_plain: Your child arranges parts of sentences and matches subjects with verbs correctly to make their writing clear and interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use specific adjectives and adverbs to enhance speech and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses specific describing words (adjectives and adverbs) to make their speaking and writing more detailed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use pronoun-antecedent agreement, including indefinite, reflexive, and relative pronouns, when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns correctly, making sure they agree with the nouns they replace when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to communicate ideas clearly and add variety to writing.
description_plain: Your child writes different types of sentences—simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex—to express ideas clearly and keep writing interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses proper English rules when speaking and writing and knows when to use formal or informal language depending on the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-and peer-edit writing for capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, paragraphing, and Standard English (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: Your child checks their own and others’ writing for correct capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, paragraphs, and proper English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing for clarity of content, word choice, sentence variety, and transition among paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by making ideas clearer, choosing better words, using different sentence types, and linking paragraphs smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear ending sentence or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expanding and embedding ideas to create sentence variety.
description_plain: Your child adds details and combines ideas in sentences to make their writing more interesting and varied.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing voice and tone by using language that provides vivid and precise vocabulary to enhance the meaning of the writing.
description_plain: Your child uses vivid and precise words to create a strong voice and tone in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using transitions within and between paragraphs to signal shifts in writing and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect ideas within and between paragraphs so their writing flows clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Defending conclusions or positions with reasons and precise, relevant evidence (e.g., facts, definitions, details, quotations, and examples).
description_plain: Your child supports their opinions or conclusions with clear reasons and specific evidence like facts, examples, or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establishing a central idea that aligns with the thesis and maintains an organized structure to fit form and topic.
description_plain: Your child writes with a clear main idea that matches their topic and organizes their writing to fit the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing a thesis statement that states a position or explains the purpose.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear thesis statement that shows their main opinion or explains the purpose of their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generate and organize ideas using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing) to develop multi-paragraph texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can come up with and organize ideas by planning, writing drafts, and making changes to create multi-paragraph pieces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write reflectively in response to reading to demonstrate thinking with details, examples, and other evidence from the text(s).
description_plain: Your child can write thoughtful responses to reading by using details and examples from the text to show their thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write persuasively supporting a well-defined point of view with appropriate claims, relevant evidence, and clear reasoning that are logically grouped.
description_plain: Your child can write to persuade others by clearly stating their point of view and supporting it with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write expository texts to examine a topic or concept that develops the focus with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, or other information from multiple credible sources, using structures and patterns (e.g., description, enumeration, classification, comparison, problem-solution, or cause-effect) to clarify relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic using facts and details from trustworthy sources, organizing ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or to alter an existing text, using a variety of precise words and phrases and transitional words to develop the characters, convey sequence, and signal shifts from one timeframe or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using descriptive words and transitions to show characters, order, and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child understands different ways and reasons to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will compose various works for diverse audiences and purposes, linked to grade seven content and texts.
description_plain: Your child will write different kinds of texts for various readers and purposes, connected to what they learn in seventh grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations or viewpoints of key information by emphasizing different facts, opinions, and reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more authors present the same topic differently by focusing on different facts, opinions, or reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze ideas within and between selections including how specific sentences, paragraphs, or sections contribute to the development and meaning of ideas.
description_plain: Your child can understand how sentences and paragraphs work together to develop ideas and meaning in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author’s purpose(s) reflects the author’s perspective (e.g., beliefs, assumptions, biases) and influences the meaning of an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s beliefs or biases affect the purpose and meaning of an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author’s word choice, organizational pattern, and language structure impact the author’s purpose and support the reader’s comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s choice of words and how the text is organized help the author’s purpose and make the text easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author uses text features (e.g., boldface and italics; type set in color; underlining; indentation; sidebars; illustrations, graphics and photographs; headings and subheadings; footnotes and annotations) to enhance and support the reader’s comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can explain how text features like bold words, pictures, headings, and notes help readers understand the information better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace the argument and specific claims in texts and assess whether all the evidence presented is relevant and whether irrelevant evidence was introduced.
description_plain: Your child can follow an argument in a text and decide if all the evidence is relevant or if some is not related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the author unfolds a perspective or series of ideas or events in historical, scientific, or technical texts, including the order in which the points are made and how they are introduced and developed.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how an author presents ideas or events in order, showing how they introduce and develop their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create a main idea statement and provide an accurate summary of how key events or ideas develop through the text.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main idea and summarize how important events or ideas develop in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands how to find key ideas and supporting details in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from grade-level informational texts to understand and learn new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories and poems or different types of stories by looking at how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how particular elements of stories or dramas interact including how settings shape and influence characters and plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story or play work together, like how the setting affects the characters and plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the points of view of different characters in a text (e.g., first-person, third person limited, third-person omniscient) and how they affect the reader’s interpretation of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters’ points of view and how these views change the way the story feels.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the elements of an author’s style (e.g., word choice, sentence structure, dialogue, figurative language, imagery) are used to influence and develop tone.
description_plain: Your child can look at how an author’s choice of words, sentences, and descriptions create the mood or feeling of a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how elements of authors’ styles (e.g., word choice, dialogue, form, voice, rhyme, rhythm, and/or sound devices) contribute to meaning in various forms of prose and poetry.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author’s style, like word choice and rhythm, helps give meaning to stories and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how static and dynamic characters and the roles of protagonist and antagonist influence plot events.
description_plain: Your child can explain how characters who change or stay the same, and the main good and bad characters, affect what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the central conflict and key elements (e.g., exposition, initiating event, rising action, climax, falling action, and resolution) impact plot development.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how the main problem and parts of the story, like the beginning and climax, shape the plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe stated or implied themes of texts and analyze their development throughout the texts using specific details.
description_plain: Your child can describe the main message or theme of a story and explain how it develops using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include short stories, literary nonfiction, novels, poetry, and drama.
description_plain: Your child will use examples from stories, poems, and plays to show they understand and learn from what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in multiple contexts, including in students’ discussions and speaking and writing activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words they learn in speaking and writing in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general and specialized word-reference materials, print and digital, to identify word origins and derivations, pronunciations, precise meanings, and their parts of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find word meanings, origins, pronunciations, and parts of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the nuances in the meaning of connotations of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the small differences in meaning between words that are similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the construction and meaning of figurative language, including simile, hyperbole, metaphor, and personification.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language like similes, metaphors, and personification.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words, including synonyms, antonyms, and analogies to better understand each word.
description_plain: Your child can use relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of Greek and Latin roots and affixes to predict the meaning of unfamiliar words.
description_plain: Your child can use knowledge of Greek and Latin word parts to figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word’s position or function in a sentence) to determine the meaning of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use the surrounding words and sentences to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and accurately use general academic language and content-specific vocabulary by listening to, reading, and discussing a variety of grade-seven texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use academic and subject-specific words by listening, reading, and talking about grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child will build their vocabulary and word skills by working with grade 7 words and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on grade seven content and texts.
description_plain: Your child will steadily grow their vocabulary and understanding of words through grade 7 reading and content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to text structure, common organizational structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies, 3-12).
description_plain: Your child can use reading strategies like summarizing and asking questions to understand difficult parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.). Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child will read related texts on the same topic to build knowledge and learn new words, sometimes reading alone or with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussion and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can find and use several pieces of evidence from texts to support their ideas when discussing or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the mid-range of the grades 6-8 band. (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different kinds of stories and information that are typical for grades 6 to 8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child will read grade-level texts smoothly and accurately, using expression, and check their understanding as they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child understands how to read challenging texts carefully, find evidence, learn new words, and use strategies when they don’t understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ethical and responsible use of all sources, including the Internet, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and new technologies, as they develop.
description_plain: Your child will use information from the internet, AI, and other technologies honestly and responsibly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite primary and secondary sources using the Modern Language Association (MLA) or American Psychological Association (APA) style.
description_plain: Your child can give credit to sources they use by following MLA or APA citation styles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and share findings in formal and informal oral or written formats.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share what they learn clearly, both by speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote, summarize, and paraphrase research findings from primary and secondary sources, avoiding plagiarism by using own words and following ethical and legal guidelines.
description_plain: Your child can quote, summarize, and explain research in their own words without copying, following rules about honesty.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and analyze the relevance, validity, and credibility of sources (primary, secondary, digital, and print) identifying main and supporting ideas, conflicting information, points of view, and any biases.
description_plain: Your child can check if sources are trustworthy and understand their main ideas, different opinions, and any bias.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collect, organize, and synthesize information from multiple sources using various notetaking formats.
description_plain: Your child can gather and organize information from many sources using different note-taking methods.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate and revise questions about a research topic, broadening or narrowing the inquiry as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can create and change questions about a topic to help guide their research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child understands how to evaluate and combine information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-eight content and texts, solve problems, and support cross-curricular learning.
description_plain: Your child will research and read related texts to learn about grade 8 topics, solve problems, and connect ideas across subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate sources for their relationships, motives, intent, and content (e.g., social, emotional, commercial).
description_plain: Your child can look at sources to understand their purpose, feelings, and reasons behind the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how similar information is presented in diverse media formats by examining electronic, print, propaganda, and mass media.
description_plain: Your child can compare how similar information is shown in different types of media like print, electronic, and ads.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how the media can influence beliefs, behaviors, and interpretations by using persuasive techniques (e.g., name calling, innuendo, glittering generalities, card stacking, bandwagon, testimonials, appeal to prestige, snobbery or plain folks, appeal to emotions).
description_plain: Your child understands how media uses tricks like name-calling or emotional appeals to influence what people think and do.

subject: Language Arts
description: Craft and publish audience-specific media messages that present claims and findings with relevant evidence and reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can create and share messages for specific audiences that include clear claims and supporting evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Emphasizes different points of view.
description_plain: Your child includes different points of view when sharing information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Uses two or more communication modes to make meaning (e.g., still or moving images, gestures, spoken language, and written language).
description_plain: Your child uses two or more ways to communicate, like pictures, gestures, speaking, or writing, to explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Uses pertinent descriptions, facts, and details.
description_plain: Your child uses important descriptions, facts, and details to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and present a multimodal presentation that
description_plain: Your child plans and gives a presentation using different types of media to share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child can use different types of media, like text, images, and videos, to understand and share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Referencing source material as appropriate during the presentation.
description_plain: Your child will mention where information comes from when giving a presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Responding to audience questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions and respond to comments using facts and ideas that relate to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using language and vocabulary appropriate to the audience, topic, and purpose, including speaking clearly at an understandable pace with appropriate tone and volume.
description_plain: Your child will speak clearly using words and tone that fit the audience and topic, and talk at a pace that others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Incorporating pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples to support the main ideas.
description_plain: Your child can include important details, facts, and examples to support their main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Clearly communicating information in an organized and succinct manner.
description_plain: Your child will share information in a clear and organized way without extra or confusing details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Deliver collaborative and individual formal and informal interactive presentations This includes:
description_plain: Your child can give both group and solo presentations, formal or informal, and take part in interactive talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child will practice speaking and sharing ideas clearly in presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using self-reflection to evaluate one’s own role in preparation and participation in small-group activities.
description_plain: Your child can think about how well they prepared and participated in small group work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Valuing contributions made by each group member while exhibiting a willingness to make necessary concessions to accomplish a common goal.
description_plain: Your child will appreciate what each group member adds and be willing to compromise to reach a shared goal.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledging new insights expressed by others, and, when justified, modifying their own views.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new ideas from others and change their own opinions when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Thoughtfully paraphrasing and summarizing ideas made during discussions.
description_plain: Your child will restate and summarize ideas shared during group discussions thoughtfully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicating agreement or tactful disagreement with others’ ideas, using carefully constructed responses.
description_plain: Your child can agree or disagree politely with others by using respectful and clear responses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Asking clarifying questions and responding appropriately to others’ questions to encourage discussion, foster understanding, and maintain focus on the topic.
description_plain: Your child will ask questions to make things clearer and answer others’ questions to keep the discussion focused and helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Working effectively and respectfully by actively contributing relevant and wellsupported ideas and opinions and sharing responsibility for the collaborative work.
description_plain: Your child can work well with others by sharing good ideas and helping with the group’s work respectfully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listening actively, offering and seeking ideas, and setting guidelines for presentations and discussions.
description_plain: Your child will listen carefully, share ideas, and help set rules for group talks and presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Facilitate and contribute to a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade eight topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can lead and take part in long group discussions with different people about grade 8 topics and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child will practice communication, listening, and teamwork skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child will learn to speak and work well with others to understand and talk about what they are learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other tools to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and consistently spell frequently used words accurately.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and spell common words correctly every time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use and punctuate dialogue and direct quotations appropriately in writing.
description_plain: Your child can use quotation marks and punctuation correctly when writing dialogue or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct complete sentences with appropriate punctuation, avoiding comma splices and run-ons in writing.
description_plain: Your child can write complete sentences with correct punctuation and avoid run-on sentences or comma mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistent verb tense across paragraphs in writing.
description_plain: Your child keeps the same verb tense throughout paragraphs when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Arrange phrases and clauses within a sentence to improve meaning reader/listener interest, and style in writing.
description_plain: Your child arranges parts of sentences to make their writing clearer and more interesting to read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use specific adjectives and adverbs to enhance speech and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses specific describing words (adjectives and adverbs) to make speaking and writing more vivid.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use pronoun-antecedent agreement, including indefinite, reflexive, and relative pronouns, when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns correctly, making sure they match the nouns they replace when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to communicate ideas clearly and add variety to writing.
description_plain: Your child writes different types of sentences (simple, compound, complex) to share ideas clearly and keep writing interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar and knows when to speak or write formally or informally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-and peer-edit writing for capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, paragraphing, and Standard English (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: Your child checks their own and others’ writing for correct capitalization, spelling, punctuation, sentence structure, paragraphs, and grammar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing for clarity of content, word choice, sentence variety, and transition among paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by making ideas clearer, choosing better words, varying sentences, and linking paragraphs smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Providing a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child adds a clear ending statement or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expanding and embedding ideas to create sentence variety.
description_plain: Your child expands ideas and mixes sentence types to make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing voice and tone by using language that provides vivid and precise vocabulary to enhance the meaning of the writing.
description_plain: Your child uses strong and precise words to create a clear voice and tone in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using appropriate and varied transitions to signal shifts in writing to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to show how ideas connect and change in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Stating and defending conclusions or positions with reasons and precise, relevant evidence and complete explanation of how evidence and details support a position addressing counterclaims when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child states opinions or conclusions and supports them with clear reasons and evidence, including addressing opposing views when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establishing a central idea that aligns with the thesis and maintaining an organized structure and formal style to fit form and topic, providing elaboration and unity throughout the writing and maintaining a consistent point of view.
description_plain: Your child creates a main idea that fits their topic and keeps their writing organized, formal, and focused from start to finish.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing a thesis statement that states a position or explains the purpose.
description_plain: Your child writes a thesis statement that clearly shows their position or purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generate and organize ideas using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing) to develop multi-paragraph texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child plans, drafts, revises, and edits to write multi-paragraph texts with organized ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write reflectively in response to reading to demonstrate thinking with details, examples, and other evidence from the text(s).
description_plain: Your child writes thoughtful responses to reading using details and examples from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write persuasively, supporting well-defined points of view effectively with relevant evidence and clear reasoning in ways that logically advance the claim(s) made.
description_plain: Your child can write to convince others by clearly stating their opinion and supporting it with good reasons and facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write expository texts to examine a topic or concept that conveys ideas and information by maintaining a clear focus with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, and examples from multiple credible sources, using structures and patterns (e.g., description, enumeration, classification, comparison, problem-solution, or causeeffect) to clarify relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that explain a topic clearly using facts, examples, and details from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or to alter an existing text, using well-structured event sequences, precise words and phrases, and transitional words to develop the characters, convey sequence, and capture the action.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that show real or imagined events with clear order, strong word choices, and smooth transitions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child understands different ways to write and the reasons for writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will compose various works for diverse audiences and purposes, linked to grade eight content and texts.
description_plain: Your child will write different types of work for various readers and purposes, connected to what they learn in eighth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how two or more authors present conflicting information on the same topic by assessing where the texts disagree in reasoning and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain differences in how two or more authors present opposing information on the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze ideas within and between selections including how specific sentences, paragraphs, sections, and text features contribute to the development and refinement of the ideas presented.
description_plain: Your child can look closely at parts of a text to understand how they help develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author establishes and conveys a perspective or purpose in a text and acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author shows their point of view and responds to opposing ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author’s word choice, organizational pattern, language structure, viewpoints, and qualifications impact meaning and credibility of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s word choices and how they organize their writing affect the meaning and trustworthiness of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate an author’s use of text features (e.g., boldface and italics; type set in color; underlining; indentation; sidebars; illustrations, graphics, and photographs; headings and subheadings; footnotes and annotations) to enhance and support the reader’s comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can use things like bold words, pictures, and headings in a text to better understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning and evidence are relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow an argument in a text and decide if the reasons and facts really support the author’s claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the author unfolds a perspective or series of ideas or events in historical, scientific, or technical texts, including the order in which the points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections drawn between them.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author presents ideas or events step-by-step and connects them in history, science, or technical writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create a main idea statement and provide an accurate summary, clarifying the relationships among the key details and ideas or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a main idea statement and summarize a text by showing how key details and ideas relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands how to find main ideas and supporting details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence from reading to understand and learn from challenging informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories; literary nonfiction and informational) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare different types of stories or poems to see how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast fictional portrayals of a time, place, or character with historical accounts of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare made-up stories about a time or place with real historical accounts to understand how fiction changes history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more stories, poems, and plays and analyze how the differing structure of each literary text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare the structure of two or more stories, poems, or plays and explain how their different forms affect their meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author’s use of literary devices including foreshadowing, flashback, symbolism, and irony are used to build mystery, suspense, or surprise.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors use things like foreshadowing, flashbacks, symbols, and irony to create surprise or suspense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters’ or narrators’ points of view and how they compare.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the elements of an author’s style (e.g., dialogue, sentence structure and word choices) are used to influence and develop tone and voice.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s style, like the way they write dialogue or choose words, helps create the mood and personality of the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret the meaning and analyze the impact of poetic elements in prose and poetry (e.g., rhyme, rhythm, repetition, alliteration, and onomatopoeia) on the reader.
description_plain: Your child understands how poetic features like rhyme, rhythm, and repetition affect how a poem or story feels to the reader.

subject: Language Arts
description: Justify the decisions and actions of dynamic versus static characters using dialogue or specific events from the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain why characters change or stay the same by using examples from what they say or do in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how different plot patterns (e.g., comedy, drama, tragedy) and subplots create meaning and examine their influence on characters, theme, pacing, and point of view.
description_plain: Your child can describe how different types of story plots and smaller storylines affect the characters, theme, pace, and point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and explain the development of theme(s) over the course of texts and their relationship to the characters, settings, plots, and overall messages.
description_plain: Your child understands how the main message or theme of a story develops through the characters, setting, and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include short stories, literary nonfiction, novels, poetry, and drama.
description_plain: Your child uses examples from the text to show they understand and learn from different kinds of stories, poems, and plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in multiple contexts, including in students’ discussions and speaking and writing activities.
description_plain: Your child uses new words they learn in speaking, writing, and discussions in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general and specialized word-reference materials, print and digital, to determine pronunciation, etymology, derivations, and parts of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries and other resources to find out how to say words, where they come from, and what parts of speech they are.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discriminate between the meanings of connotative words and their denotative meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the literal meaning of a word and the feelings or ideas it suggests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the construction and meaning of an author’s use of symbols, analogy, and figurative language such as simile, metaphor, personification, hyperbole, and idiom.
description_plain: Your child understands how authors use symbols and comparisons like similes and metaphors to add meaning to their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words, including synonyms, antonyms, and analogies to better understand each word.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of Greek and Latin roots and affixes to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of Greek and Latin word parts to figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word’s position or function in a sentence) to determine the meaning of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from sentences and paragraphs to understand the meaning of unfamiliar words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and accurately use general and academic language and content-specific vocabulary by listening to, reading, and discussing a variety of grade-eight texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses general and subject-specific vocabulary by listening, reading, and talking about grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child builds their vocabulary and word skills by learning new words and how they work.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on grade eight content and texts.
description_plain: Your child steadily grows their vocabulary and word knowledge through grade 8 reading and content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to text structure, common organizational structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies, 3-12).
description_plain: Your child uses reading strategies like summarizing and asking questions to understand difficult parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary. (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.) Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads related texts on the same topic at different difficulty levels to build knowledge and learn new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussion and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child supports their ideas in discussions or writing by using several examples and quotes from grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the higher range of the grades 6-8 band (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts that are appropriate for grades 6-8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can read challenging texts aloud smoothly and with expression, checking and fixing any mistakes to understand the meaning better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child will read different difficult texts to learn new information and words, and use strategies to understand when the reading gets hard.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ethical and responsible use of all sources, including the Internet, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and new technologies, as they develop.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use all sources, including the internet and AI, in a responsible and honest way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define plagiarism's meaning and legal consequences and follow ethical and legal guidelines for gathering and using information.
description_plain: Your child knows what plagiarism is, understands its consequences, and follows rules to gather and use information honestly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite sources for quoted and paraphrased ideas using a standard method of documentation, such as the Modern Language Association (MLA) or the American Psychological Association (APA).
description_plain: Your child can give credit to sources when using quotes or ideas by following standard citation methods like MLA or APA.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create research products aligned with the demands of the reading and writing standards.
description_plain: Your child can create research projects that meet reading and writing expectations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize multiple streams of information to support claims and introduce counterclaims.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources to support their ideas and also present opposing views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and evaluate the primary and secondary sources gathered for their credibility, reliability, accuracy, and usefulness that includes identifying their main and supporting ideas, points of view, conflicting information, and any misconceptions or biases.
description_plain: Your child can check if sources are trustworthy and useful by finding main ideas, opinions, conflicts, and any bias.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather and organize information from various sources, including internet resources, electronic databases, and other technology.
description_plain: Your child can find and organize information from many places like websites, databases, and other technology.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate and revise questions about a research topic, broadening or narrowing the inquiry as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can ask and change questions about a research topic to make it broader or more focused.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child understands how to evaluate and combine information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-nine content, texts and areas prompted by student interest.
description_plain: Your child will research and read related texts on topics to learn more about ninth-grade subjects and their interests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the possible cause and effect relationships between mass media coverage and public opinion trends in media messages.
description_plain: Your child can explain how news coverage can affect what people think and feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate sources including advertisements, editorials, political cartoons, and feature stories for relationships between intent and factual content.
description_plain: Your child can judge ads, editorials, cartoons, and stories to see how their purpose matches the facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how values and viewpoints are included or excluded and how the media can influence beliefs, behaviors, and interpretations.
description_plain: Your child can look at how media includes or leaves out certain values and views, and how this affects beliefs and actions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the credibility, word choice, viewpoints, and bias in media presentations.
description_plain: Your child can evaluate if media is trustworthy by looking at word choices, opinions, and bias.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the persuasive techniques used in diverse media formats (e.g., name calling, innuendo, glittering generalities, card stacking, bandwagon, testimonials, appeal to prestige, snobbery or plain folks, appeal to emotion).
description_plain: Your child can identify different ways media tries to persuade people, like using emotions or popular opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the purpose of the media message and its effect on the audience.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why a media message was made and how it influences the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create media messages for diverse audiences and purposes.
description_plain: Your child can create media messages that fit different audiences and purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Monitor, analyze, and use multiple streams of simultaneous information.
description_plain: Your child can watch, think about, and use information coming from many sources at the same time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of multimodal tools.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of media and tools, like videos or images, to help share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child understands how to combine reading, writing, speaking, and using media to learn and communicate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Memorize and accurately recite a short selection from a longer text demonstrating inflection and meaningful expression that is appropriate to the tone and voice of the selection.
description_plain: Your child can memorize and recite a short part of a longer text, using the right tone and expression to match the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyzing the effectiveness of one’s presentation, including introduction, central idea, organization, and conclusion.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain how well their presentation worked, including the introduction, main idea, order, and ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using listening and speaking strategies effectively with awareness of verbal and nonverbal cues (e.g., using body language to indicate attentiveness, and giving appropriate feedback).
description_plain: Your child uses good listening and speaking skills, paying attention to body language and giving helpful responses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choosing vocabulary, language and tone appropriate to the topic, audience, and purpose.
description_plain: Your child picks words and tone that fit the topic, audience, and purpose when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an opinion. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly about a topic or text and share their opinion with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and presenting ideas clearly to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using reflection to evaluate one’s own role and the process in paired or small-group activities.
description_plain: Your child thinks about their own role and how the group worked during small group activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarizing points of agreement and disagreement.
description_plain: Your child can summarize what the group agrees and disagrees on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Responding thoughtfully and tactfully with evidence to diverse perspectives, including challenging, clarifying, or verifying ideas and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child responds thoughtfully and respectfully to different opinions, using evidence to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Setting clear goals and deadlines and defining individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child helps set clear goals, deadlines, and roles for group work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Incorporating all group members in the development of new understandings, making decisions, and solving problems.
description_plain: Your child includes everyone in the group when making decisions and solving problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Setting guidelines for group presentations and discussions.
description_plain: Your child helps create rules for group presentations and discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Applying a variety of strategies to listen actively and speak purposefully and respectfully.
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to listen carefully and speak clearly and respectfully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Facilitate and contribute to a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade nine topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child takes part in and helps lead group discussions on grade-level topics and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child practices communication, listening, and working well with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child learns to speak and work with others to understand and share ideas together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly, consulting reference materials to check as needed.
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly and checks spelling using reference materials when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply a style manual, such as that of the Modern Language Association (MLA) or the American Psychological Association (APA) to apply rules for citing sources.
description_plain: Your child learns to follow rules from style guides like MLA or APA to show where information comes from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and semicolons to distinguish and divide main and subordinate clauses to link two or more closely related independent clauses when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and semicolons correctly to join and separate parts of sentences when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistent verb tense when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child will keep verb tenses consistent when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appositives and main and subordinate clauses to convey and clarify a message when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can use extra information and different types of clauses to make their message clear when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use and apply the active and passive voice as appropriate when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child understands when to use active or passive voice in speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Craft and apply a variety of sentence structures to infuse sentence variety in writing.
description_plain: Your child can write sentences in different ways to make their writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use parallel structure across sentences and paragraphs to link and compare/contrast ideas in writing and speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses matching sentence patterns to connect and compare ideas when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English rules when speaking and writing and knows when to use formal or informal language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Edit writing for appropriate conventions, style, and language (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: Your child can check and fix their writing to make sure it follows rules and sounds clear and correct.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use peer- and self-evaluation to edit writing for clarity and quality of information, addressing strengths and making suggestions regarding how writing might be improved.
description_plain: Your child uses feedback from themselves and others to improve their writing by making it clearer and better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing for clarity of content, accuracy, and adequate elaboration.
description_plain: Your child revises their writing to make sure the ideas are clear, correct, and fully explained.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identifying and addressing counterarguments and providing a rebuttal where appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find opposing ideas and respond to them when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using background knowledge to expand ideas and add depth, utilizing reference materials when necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know and research to add more details and depth to their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using transitions, precise vocabulary, and sentence variety to create a cohesive structure that shows the relationship between arguments, evidence, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses connecting words, exact vocabulary, and different sentence types to organize their writing clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establishing, supporting, and maintaining a central idea with evidence throughout a piece of writing, organizing ideas in a logical sequence to exhibit unity.
description_plain: Your child keeps a main idea throughout their writing and supports it with evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing a thesis statement that clearly communicates the writer’s position or assertion.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear statement that shows their main opinion or point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and organize writing to address a specific audience and purpose using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing). This includes:
description_plain: Your child plans and organizes their writing for a specific audience and purpose by following steps like planning, drafting, revising, and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely producing shorter and longer pieces for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences (e.g., summaries, reflections, descriptions, critiques, letters, poetry, narratives, etc.).
description_plain: Your child practices writing both short and long pieces for different reasons and audiences, like stories, letters, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write reflectively in response to readings in which students compare two or more texts with details, examples, and other textual evidence to support an idea or position.
description_plain: Your child writes about what they read by comparing two or more texts using details and examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that fits with the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic through sustained use of the most significant and relevant facts, concrete details, quotations, or other information from multiple authoritative sources appropriate to the audience's knowledge.
description_plain: Your child develops their topic by using important facts, details, quotes, or information from trusted sources that fit the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adopt an organizational structure that clarifies relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can organize their writing so that the ideas and connections between them are clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly by providing context, presenting well-defined theses, and previewing what follows.
description_plain: Your child can start writing by clearly introducing the topic, giving background, stating their main point, and telling what will come next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child understands different ways to write and the reasons for writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will write in various forms for diverse audiences and purposes linked to grade nine content and texts with an emphasis on expository and persuasive writing.
description_plain: Your child will write in different styles for different readers and purposes, focusing especially on explaining ideas and convincing others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the clarity and accuracy of information found in informational texts, corroborating or challenging conclusions with other sources of information.
description_plain: Your child can check if information in nonfiction texts is clear and correct by comparing it with other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare the perspectives and viewpoints of two or more authors regarding their treatment of the same or similar topics, including the details they include and emphasize in their respective accounts as well as the impact of each author’s qualifications.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more authors view the same topic, noticing what details they focus on and how their backgrounds affect their views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how authors use rhetorical devices to create ethos, logos, and pathos and impact the reader.
description_plain: Your child understands how authors use special techniques to build trust, logic, and emotions to influence readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze an author’s word choice and use of rhetorical devices to persuade or convince an audience.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s choice of words and techniques try to persuade or convince the reader.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare characteristics of expository, technical, and persuasive texts, including their differences in purpose, format, and text structure.
description_plain: Your child can tell the differences between explaining, technical, and persuasive texts by looking at their purpose, format, and structure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among, facts, reasoned judgments, and/or speculation in texts to determine where a position/argument is to be confirmed, disproved, or modified.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and guesses in texts to decide if an argument is right, wrong, or needs change.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the purpose and interpret the use of data and information in maps, charts, graphs, timelines, tables, and diagrams in informational, historical, scientific, or technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain why data like maps, charts, and graphs are used and what they show in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the development of main ideas over the course of texts, including how they emerge, are shaped, and are refined by specific details to help reveal the author’s intended purpose for writing.
description_plain: Your child can follow how main ideas develop in a text and how details help explain the author’s purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands how to find important ideas and supporting details in nonfiction texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence from complex nonfiction texts to understand and learn new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships between and among particular literary elements of a story or play, including how the setting shapes the plot and characters.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story or play, like the setting, affect the plot and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how the historical or social function of a text depends on its context (e.g., cultural, situational, historical, geographical).
description_plain: Your child understands how a text’s meaning depends on its historical, cultural, or social background.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the point of view and distinguish between what is implied or intended because of the use of hyperbole, irony, sarcasm, and understatement.
description_plain: Your child can explain the point of view in a story and tell when the author uses exaggeration, irony, sarcasm, or understatement to make a point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author’s specific word choices, syntax, tone, and voice shape the meaning of the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s word choices, sentence style, tone, and voice affect the meaning of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the use of rhyme, rhythm, sound, imagery, and other literary devices in poetry to convey a message and elicit a reader’s emotions.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how poetry uses rhyme, rhythm, sounds, and images to share a message and create feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and describe how dramatic conventions (e.g., soliloquy, aside, narration, direct address to the audience) contribute to the theme and effect of plays from various cultures.
description_plain: Your child can identify how special parts of plays, like soliloquies or talking to the audience, help show the theme and create effects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between character types in literary texts (e.g., dynamic/round character, static/flat character, and stereotype) and their impact on the theme.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between types of characters in stories, like those who change and those who stay the same, and explain how these characters affect the story’s main message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine and analyze the characteristics that distinguish literary forms (e.g., fiction, nonfiction, poetry, prose, novel, drama, essay, speech) and analyze how the differing structure of each literary form contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different kinds of writing, such as stories, poems, essays, and speeches, and explain how their unique styles help show their meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain stated or implied themes, analyzing their development over the course of texts, and the relationship of characters, setting, and plot to those themes.
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas or messages in stories and can explain how characters, places, and events help develop these ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include narratives, literary nonfiction, poetry, and drama.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from different kinds of complex stories and poems to understand and learn from them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in multiple contexts, including students’ discussions and speaking and writing activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases in speaking and writing, and during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of literary and classical allusions and figurative language in context and analyze their roles in texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain the meaning of references to famous stories or ideas and understand special language like metaphors in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain idiomatic language in context.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common expressions that don’t mean exactly what the words say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discriminate between the connotative and denotative meanings and interpret the connotation(s).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the basic meaning of a word and the feelings or ideas it suggests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use structural analysis of roots, affixes, and etymology to explain the meanings of unfamiliar and complex words.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of hard words by looking at their parts and origins.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context and sentence structure to clarify the literal and figurative meanings of words and phrases.
