description_plain: Your child can use clues from sentences and context to understand both the literal and hidden meanings of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and accurately use general academic and content-specific vocabulary through reading, discussing, and writing about grade-level texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses important academic and subject-specific words by reading, talking, and writing about grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child works on building vocabulary and understanding words better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on grade nine content and texts.
description_plain: Your child steadily grows their vocabulary and word knowledge through reading and learning grade-level materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to text structure, common organizational structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies, 3-12).
description_plain: Your child uses helpful reading strategies, like summarizing and asking questions, to understand difficult parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.). Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads several related texts on a topic to build knowledge and learn new words, sometimes reading alone or with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussions and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child supports their ideas in discussions or writing by using several pieces of evidence from grade-level texts, including quoting or explaining where they found the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the lower range of the grades 9-10 band. (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand a variety of stories and informational texts that are appropriate for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts accurately and smoothly, paying attention to meaning and fixing mistakes when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child builds knowledge and understanding by reading challenging texts, reading widely on topics, and using strategies when they have trouble understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ethical and responsible use of all sources, including the Internet, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and new technologies, as they develop.
description_plain: Your child learns to use all sources, like the internet and AI tools, in honest and responsible ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define the meaning and legal consequences of plagiarism and follow ethical and legal guidelines for gathering and using information.
description_plain: Your child understands what plagiarism is, why it’s wrong, and how to use information honestly and legally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite primary and secondary sources for quoted and paraphrased ideas using a standard method of documentation, such as the Modern Language Association (MLA) or American Psychological Association (APA).
description_plain: Your child can show where they found information by citing sources correctly using common styles like MLA or APA.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create research products aligned with the demands of the reading and writing standards.
description_plain: Your child creates research projects that meet reading and writing expectations for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize multiple streams of information from a variety of sources to support claims and introduce counterclaims.
description_plain: Your child combines information from different sources to support their ideas and also presents opposing views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Objectively evaluate primary and secondary sources for their credibility, reliability, accuracy, usefulness, and limitations, that includes identifying their main and supporting ideas, points of view, conflicting information, and any misconceptions or biases.
description_plain: Your child can judge if sources are trustworthy and useful by looking at their main ideas, opinions, and any biases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather and organize information from various sources, including internet resources, electronic databases, and other technology.
description_plain: Your child gathers and organizes information from many places, including the internet and electronic databases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate and revise questions about a research topic, broadening or narrowing the inquiry as necessary.
description_plain: Your child asks and changes questions about a topic to make their research clearer or more focused.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child learns how to evaluate and combine information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-ten content, texts, and areas prompted by student interest.
description_plain: Your child researches and reads related texts on topics to build knowledge based on their grade level and interests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe possible cause-and- effect relationships between mass media coverage and public opinion trends.
description_plain: Your child explains how news coverage can influence what people think and feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, and political) behind media messages used to determine author’s purpose, factual content, opinion, and/or possible bias.
description_plain: Your child looks at why media messages are made, such as for social, business, or political reasons, to understand the author’s purpose and any bias.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine and analyze how media messages are constructed based on varying opinions, values, and viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child studies how media messages are created based on different opinions and values.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze, compare, and contrast visual and verbal media messages for content (e.g., word choice and choice of information), intent (e.g., persuasive techniques, including but not limited to ad hominem, red herring, and strawman), impact (e.g., public opinion trends), and effectiveness (e.g., effect on the audience).
description_plain: Your child compares pictures and words in media to understand their message, purpose, impact, and how well they work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the viewpoint of print and digital publications (e.g., advertisements, editorials, blogs, and websites).
description_plain: Your child analyzes the viewpoint in print and online materials like ads, editorials, blogs, and websites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create media messages for diverse audiences and purposes.
description_plain: Your child creates media messages designed for different audiences and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Monitor, analyze, and use multiple streams of simultaneous information.
description_plain: Your child watches and thinks about several streams of information happening at the same time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of multimodal tools, including using information from two or more interdependent modes of communication where both or multiple modes are essential to convey the intended message (e.g., graphics, moving images, music).
description_plain: Your child uses tools like pictures, videos, and music together to share a clear message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to use different types of media together to understand and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Memorize and accurately recite a speech with intonation, meaningful expression, and emotion that conveys the intended mood (e.g., inspiration, motivation, conviction).
description_plain: Your child memorizes and performs a speech with expression and feeling to show the right mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluating the effectiveness of presentations, including the introduction, central ideas, organization, and conclusion.
description_plain: Your child evaluates how well a presentation works by looking at its introduction, main ideas, organization, and ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using active listening and speaking strategies effectively with awareness of verbal and nonverbal cues (e.g., appropriate facial expressions and posture).
description_plain: Your child can listen and speak carefully, noticing and using body language like facial expressions and posture.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choosing vocabulary, language, and tone appropriate to the topic, audience, and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can choose words and tone that fit the topic, who they are talking to, and why they are speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an opinion. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can give an oral report on a topic or text and share their opinion clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child practices speaking and sharing ideas clearly in front of others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using reflection to evaluate one's own role in the process in pairs or small-group activities.
description_plain: Your child thinks about and talks about their own role when working with a partner or small group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assessing, evaluating critically, and using information accurately for a common purpose or goal.
description_plain: Your child looks carefully at information and uses it correctly to help the group reach a goal.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarizing points of agreement and disagreement.
description_plain: Your child can explain what the group agrees on and what they disagree on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Responding thoughtfully, respectfully, and tactfully with evidence to diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child responds to different opinions politely and with good reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Setting clear goals and deadlines and defining individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child helps set clear goals, deadlines, and roles for group work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Incorporating all group members in the development of new understandings, making decisions, and solving problems.
description_plain: Your child includes everyone in the group when making decisions and solving problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Setting guidelines for group presentations and discussions.
description_plain: Your child helps create rules for group talks and presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Applying a variety of strategies to listen actively and speak purposefully and respectfully.
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to listen carefully and speak clearly and respectfully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Facilitate and contribute to a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade ten topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child joins and leads group discussions on grade 10 topics with different people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child practices talking, listening, and working well with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child learns to speak and work with others to understand and share ideas together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly, consulting reference materials to check as needed.
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly and checks in books or online when unsure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply a style manual, such as that of the Modern Language Association (MLA) or the American Psychological Association (APA), to apply rules for punctuation and formatting of direct quotations in writing.
description_plain: Your child follows rules from style guides like MLA or APA for punctuation and formatting quotes in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply the rules for the use of a colon (e.g., joining independent clauses, introducing a list, introducing a quotation) when writing.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use colons correctly in writing, like before lists or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistent verb tense when speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child keeps verb tenses the same when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use subordination, coordination, apposition, and other devices to indicate the relationship between ideas clearly.
description_plain: Your child uses words and punctuation to clearly show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use active and passive voice to convey a desired effect in speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between active and passive voice and use them to make speaking and writing more effective.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use complex sentence structure (made up of main and subordinate clauses and subordinating conjunctions) to infuse sentence variety in writing.
description_plain: Your child will use complex sentences with main and subordinate parts to make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use, edit, and revise parallel structure across complex sentences and paragraphs in writing.
description_plain: Your child can use and fix parallel sentence structures to make writing clearer and more balanced.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child understands when to use formal English and when informal language is okay in speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Edit writing for appropriate conventions, style, and language (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: Your child can check and fix writing to use correct grammar, style, and language for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use peer- and self-evaluation to edit writing for clarity and quality of information, addressing strengths and making suggestions regarding how writing might be improved.
description_plain: Your child uses feedback from themselves and others to improve their writing’s clarity and quality.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing for clarity of content, accuracy, and adequate elaboration.
description_plain: Your child revises writing to make ideas clear, accurate, and well explained.

subject: Language Arts
description: Elaborating ideas clearly through intentional word choice and varied sentence structure.
description_plain: Your child explains ideas clearly by choosing words carefully and using different sentence types.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using transitions effectively to connect ideas within and across paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to connect ideas within and between paragraphs smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Embedding narrative techniques (e.g., anecdotes, dialogue, and description) to develop and enhance writing.
description_plain: Your child adds stories, dialogue, and descriptions to make writing more interesting and detailed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Defending a position using sufficient reasons with evidence from credible sources as support.
description_plain: Your child supports their opinions with good reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introducing and developing central idea(s), and organizing ideas in a logical sequence to exhibit unity.
description_plain: Your child introduces main ideas and arranges them in a clear, logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing a thesis statement that clearly communicates the writer’s position or assertion.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear thesis statement that shows their main point or opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and organize writing to address a specific audience and purpose using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing). This includes:
description_plain: Your child plans and organizes writing for a specific audience and purpose by following steps like planning, drafting, revising, and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely producing shorter and longer pieces that adapt writing content, technique, and voice for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences, (e.g., summaries, reflections, descriptions, critiques, letters, poetry, narratives, etc.).
description_plain: Your child practices writing different lengths and types of pieces, like summaries, letters, poems, or stories, adjusting style and voice for different tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write reflectively in response to readings in which students compare two or more texts with details, examples, and other textual evidence to support an idea or position.
description_plain: Your child writes thoughtful responses comparing two or more texts using details and examples to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide conclusions that follow from and support the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes conclusions that clearly support the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Address and refute counterclaims.
description_plain: Your child recognizes opposing views and explains why their own opinion is stronger.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support well-defined points of view effectively with relevant evidence and clear reasoning in ways that logically advance the claim(s).
description_plain: Your child supports clear opinions with relevant evidence and logical reasons to strengthen their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop a thesis that demonstrates knowledgeable judgements.
description_plain: Your child creates a strong thesis that shows they understand the topic well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear ending that sums up the information or explanation they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic through sustained use of the most significant and relevant facts, concrete details, quotations, or other information from multiple authoritative sources appropriate to the audience’s knowledge.
description_plain: Your child will support their topic with important facts, details, quotes, or information from several trustworthy sources that fit the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adopt an organizational structure that clarifies relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can organize their writing so the ideas and connections are easy to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly by providing context, presenting well-defined theses, and previewing what follows.
description_plain: Your child will start writing by clearly introducing the topic, giving background, stating their main idea, and showing what will come next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child understands different ways to write and why they write in those ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will write in a variety of forms for diverse audiences and purposes linked to grade ten content and texts with an emphasis on argumentative writing.
description_plain: Your child will write in many styles for different readers and reasons, focusing especially on writing arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple texts addressing the same topic to determine how authors reach similar or different conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can read several texts on the same topic and explain how the authors come to similar or different conclusions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how different authors write about the same topic and shape their presentations or viewpoints of key information using facts, opinions, and reasoning.
description_plain: Your child will notice how different authors write about the same topic and use facts, opinions, and reasons to share their views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author’s purpose and impact of literary techniques such as hyperbole, analogy, and paradox as they appear in texts.
description_plain: Your child can understand why authors use special language tricks like exaggeration, comparisons, or contradictions and how these affect the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze key terms (e.g., words and phrases, technical terminology) and ideas of historical, scientific, and technical texts to clarify the relationships and understandings among key concepts.
description_plain: Your child will figure out the meaning of important words and ideas in history, science, or technical texts to better understand how things connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how authors use structure to explain relationships among concepts in a text, including how key sentences, paragraphs, and sections of texts contribute to the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors organize their writing to show how ideas relate, including how sentences and paragraphs work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the argument and specific claims in texts, examining whether the reasoning is valid, the evidence is relevant, and whether there are any false or unsupported statements.
description_plain: Your child will check if the arguments and claims in a text make sense, have good evidence, and don’t include false or unsupported statements.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare characteristics of the information from informational, historical, scientific, and technical texts and interpret the use of data and information in maps, charts, graphs, timelines, tables, and diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can compare information from different types of texts and understand how data in maps, charts, graphs, timelines, tables, and diagrams is used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how authors organize an analysis or series of ideas or events, including the order in which points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections that are drawn among them.
description_plain: Your child will explain how authors organize their ideas or events, including the order and connections between points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands main ideas and supporting details in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence from complex informational texts to understand and learn new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the similarities and differences represented in the literature of different cultures and eras.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories from different cultures and time periods to find similarities and differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast character development, dramatic plot structure, and conventions in a play to character development, narrative structure, and conventions in other literary forms.
description_plain: Your child will compare how characters grow and how stories are told in plays versus other kinds of literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain and analyze the influence of the historical and cultural context of a text on its form, style, characters, and point of view.
description_plain: Your child understands how the time and culture when a story was written affect its style, characters, and point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze point of view and distinguish between what is directly stated in a text from what is implied or intended because of the use of satire, irony, sarcasm, and understatement.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between what a text says directly and what it suggests using humor, irony, sarcasm, or understatement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how authors use specific word choices, syntax, tone, and voice to convey the author’s intent and viewpoint.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s choice of words, sentence style, tone, and voice show what the author wants to say and their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how authors use literary devices and figurative language, including allusion, allegory, and paradox to impact the meaning of the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors use special language tools like allusions, allegories, and paradoxes to add deeper meaning to a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the overall structure of a poem, including how each successive part builds on earlier sections and how rhyme, rhythm, sound, and imagery convey a message and elicit a reader’s emotions.
description_plain: Your child understands how a poem is put together, how each part connects, and how rhyme, rhythm, sounds, and pictures in words create feelings and messages.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain how dramatic conventions (e.g., soliloquy, aside, narration, direct address to the audience) contribute to the theme and effect of plays from various cultures.
description_plain: Your child can identify parts of plays like soliloquies or asides and explain how these parts help show the play’s main ideas and effects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the different character roles in literary texts (e.g., foil, tragic, hero) and their impact on the theme.
description_plain: Your child can describe different types of characters in stories, like heroes or tragic figures, and explain how they affect the story’s main message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how authors structure texts to advance the plot, explaining how each event gives rise to the next or foreshadows a future event.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors organize events in a story so that one event leads to the next or hints at what will happen later.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the development of universal themes (e.g., survival of the fittest, coming of age, power of love) prevalent in world literature (e.g., short stories, poems, plays, novels, and literary nonfiction) of different cultures and eras.
description_plain: Your child understands common themes like growing up, survival, or love in stories, poems, and plays from different cultures and times.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include literary nonfiction (including world, British, and American literature), narratives, poetry, and drama, with an emphasis on world literature.
description_plain: Your child can use examples from various complex stories, poems, and plays to show understanding and learn new ideas, especially from world literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in multiple contexts, including in students’ discussions and speaking and writing activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases in speaking and writing in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of literary and classical allusions and figurative language in context and analyze their roles in texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain the meaning of references and figurative language in stories and understand how they add to the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain idiomatic language in context.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain phrases that don’t mean exactly what the words say, like idioms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Discriminate between the connotative and denotative meanings and interpret the connotation(s).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the basic meaning of a word and the feelings or ideas it suggests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use structural analysis of roots, affixes, and etymology to clarify the meanings of unfamiliar and complex words.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of hard words by looking at word parts like roots and prefixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context and sentence structure to clarify the literal and figurative meanings of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use the words around a tricky word and sentence clues to understand its literal or hidden meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and accurately use general academic and content-specific vocabulary through reading, discussing, and writing about grade-level texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use important academic and subject-specific words by reading, talking, and writing about grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child works on building vocabulary and understanding words better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on grade ten content and text.
description_plain: Your child will steadily grow their vocabulary and word knowledge using grade 10 reading materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to text structure, common organizational structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies, 3-12).
description_plain: Your child uses reading strategies like summarizing and asking questions to understand difficult parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.). Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads groups of related texts on a topic to build knowledge and learn new words, reading some on their own and some with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussions and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child supports their ideas in discussions and writing by finding and using several examples from grade-level texts, including quoting or explaining where the information comes from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the higher range of the grades 9-10 band (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts meant for 9th and 10th graders.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child will read grade-level texts smoothly and clearly, checking their understanding and fixing mistakes as they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child understands difficult texts by reading fluently, finding evidence, learning new words, and using strategies when they don’t understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ethical and responsible use of all sources, including the Internet, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and new technologies, as they develop.
description_plain: Your child uses all sources, including the internet and AI, honestly and responsibly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define the meaning and consequences of plagiarism and follow ethical and legal guidelines for gathering and using information.
description_plain: Your child knows what plagiarism is and follows rules to use information fairly and legally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite primary and secondary sources for quoted and paraphrased ideas using a standard method of documentation such as the Modern Language Association (MLA) or American Psychological Association (APA).
description_plain: Your child can show where they found information by citing sources using common formats like MLA or APA.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create research products aligned with the demands of the reading and writing standards.
description_plain: Your child creates research projects that meet reading and writing expectations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize multiple streams of evidence to support claims and acknowledge counterclaims.
description_plain: Your child combines different pieces of evidence to support their ideas and also recognizes opposing views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Objectively evaluate primary and secondary sources for their credibility, reliability, accuracy, usefulness, and limitations. That includes identifying their main and supporting ideas, points of view, conflicting information, and any misconceptions or biases.
description_plain: Your child checks if sources are trustworthy and looks for main ideas, opinions, conflicts, and biases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather and organize information from various sources.
description_plain: Your child gathers and organizes information from different places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate and revise questions about a research topic broadening or narrowing the inquiry as necessary.
description_plain: Your child asks and changes questions about a topic to explore it more deeply or narrowly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child learns to evaluate and combine information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-eleven content, texts, and areas prompted by student interest.
description_plain: Your child researches and reads related texts to build knowledge about topics they study or are interested in.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze, compare, and contrast visual and verbal media messages for content (e.g., word choice and choice of information), intent (e.g., persuasive techniques), impact (e.g., public opinion trends), and effectiveness (e.g., effect on the audience).
description_plain: Your child compares and analyzes pictures and words in media to understand their message, purpose, effect, and how well they work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and critique how media reach the targeted audience for specific purposes
description_plain: Your child looks at how media targets specific audiences for certain reasons and critiques how well it does this.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the sources and viewpoint(s) of publications including advertisements, editorials, blogs, and websites.
description_plain: Your child examines where media comes from and the viewpoints in ads, editorials, blogs, and websites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ethically, purposefully, and strategically incorporate multimodal tools including the Internet and varying technology.
description_plain: Your child uses technology and online tools thoughtfully and ethically to support their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Monitor, organize, analyze, and synthesize multiple streams of simultaneous information to create a summary or formulate a position.
description_plain: Your child organizes and combines information from many sources to make summaries or form opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of multimodal literacy by identifying and evaluating elements such as authorship, format, audience, content, and purpose.
description_plain: Your child understands different types of media by looking at who made it, its format, audience, content, and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create, publish, and deliver multimodal presentations and pieces aimed at a variety of audiences and with different purposes, incorporating spoken or written components.
description_plain: Your child creates and shares presentations or projects using different media types for different audiences and goals.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child can use different types of media and tools together to understand and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluating the content and effectiveness of presentations; that includes the introduction, organization, strengths/weaknesses in evidence and reasoning, and conclusion.
description_plain: Your child can judge how well a presentation is made by looking at its introduction, organization, evidence, reasoning, and conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Anticipating and addressing alternative or opposing perspectives and counterclaims using counter arguments and rebuttals, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can think about and respond to different or opposing opinions by using arguments and explanations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Utilizing rhetorical techniques (e.g., ethos, pathos, and logos), repetition, and figurative language to deliver a message.
description_plain: Your child can use ways like emotions, logic, trust, repetition, and creative language to get a message across.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using a variety of active listening and speaking strategies, with awareness of intent and impact of verbal and nonverbal cues.
description_plain: Your child can listen and speak carefully, paying attention to both words and body language to understand and be understood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choosing diction and tone appropriate to the topic, audience, and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can choose words and tone that fit the topic, who they are talking to, and the purpose of the talk.

subject: Language Arts
description: Selecting the modes and purposes for presentations and synthesizing multiple streams of information.
description_plain: Your child can pick the best ways to present information and combine different sources to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an opinion. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can speak about a topic or text and share their opinion clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child can speak and present ideas clearly to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using reflection to evaluate one's own role in the group process in small-group activities.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge their own part when working in small groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assessing, evaluating critically, and using information accurately to fulfill a task.
description_plain: Your child can find, judge, and use information correctly to complete a task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarizing points of agreement and disagreement.
description_plain: Your child can summarize where people agree and disagree in a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Responding thoughtfully and tactfully with evidence to diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can respond carefully and respectfully to different opinions using evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrating the ability to work effectively by coming to a consensus, exercising flexibility, making necessary compromises, and presenting alternate views.
description_plain: Your child can work well with others by agreeing, being flexible, making compromises, and sharing different ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Applying a variety of strategies to listen actively and speak purposefully and respectfully.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and speak clearly and respectfully using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Facilitate and contribute to a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade eleven topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can lead and join in long discussions with different people about grade 11 topics and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child can communicate, listen, and work well with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child will develop strong speaking and teamwork skills to learn and understand ideas together with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly, consulting reference materials to check as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly and use resources to check spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and edit work so that it conforms to the guidelines in style manual, such as that of the Modern Language Association (MLA) or the American Psychological Association (APA).
description_plain: Your child can write and edit their work to follow rules from style guides like MLA or APA.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas, semi-colons, and colons correctly in complex sentences in writing.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, semi-colons, and colons correctly when writing complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate and apply active and passive voice to convey a desired effect in speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child understands when to use active or passive voice to make their speaking and writing more effective.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use complex sentence structure to infuse sentence variety in writing.
description_plain: Your child can write sentences with different structures to make their writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbal phrases to achieve sentence conciseness and variety in speaking and writing.
description_plain: Your child uses verbal phrases to make their speaking and writing clear and varied.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar and knows when to speak or write formally or informally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and revise to a standard acceptable both in the workplace and in post-secondary education.
description_plain: Your child writes and revises work that meets standards for both jobs and college.

subject: Language Arts
description: Edit writing for appropriate conventions, style, and language (See Language Usage for grade level expectations).
description_plain: Your child edits their writing to fix grammar, style, and word choice mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use peer- and self-evaluation to edit writing for clarity and quality of information, addressing strengths and making suggestions regarding how writing might be improved.
description_plain: Your child uses feedback from themselves and others to improve their writing’s clarity and quality.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing for clarity of content, accuracy, and depth of information.
description_plain: Your child revises their writing to make sure the ideas are clear, accurate, and detailed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Elaborating ideas clearly through purposeful and precise word choice.
description_plain: Your child explains ideas clearly by choosing the right words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Applying varied transitions and sentence structures to connect ideas within and across paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child connects ideas smoothly using different transitions and sentence types.

subject: Language Arts
description: Effectively contextualizing evidence from sources with proper introduction and thorough explanation.
description_plain: Your child introduces evidence properly and explains it well to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organizing claims, counterclaims, and evidence in a sustained and logical sequence to exhibit unity.
description_plain: Your child organizes claims, counterclaims, and evidence in a clear and logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing a thesis statement that clearly communicates the writer’s position or assertion.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear thesis statement that shows their main opinion or point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and organize writing to address a specific audience and purpose using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing). This includes:
description_plain: Your child plans and organizes their writing for a specific audience and purpose by following steps like planning, drafting, revising, and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose appropriate modes and blend multiple forms of writing by routinely engaging in the production of shorter and longer pieces that adapt writing content, technique, and voice for a range of audiences, purposes, and tasks (e.g. summaries, reflections, descriptions, critiques, letters, poetry, narratives, etc.).
description_plain: Your child writes different types of pieces, like summaries or stories, and changes their style to fit different audiences and purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to describe personal qualifications for potential occupational or educational opportunities, producing clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style match the intended audience and purpose of the workplace and/or post-secondary education and language in informal and formal contexts.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly about their skills and experiences for jobs or school, using the right style for formal or informal situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize claims, counterclaims, and evidence in a sustained and logical sequence that explains how the credible evidence supports well-defined points of view.
description_plain: Your child organizes claims, counterclaims, and evidence in a clear way that shows how the evidence supports their views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine and evaluate processes and/or problems to propose solutions.
description_plain: Your child looks at problems or processes carefully and suggests solutions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret and investigate evidence from various sources and texts to draw reasonable conclusions that support the writer’s position or assertion.
description_plain: Your child finds and uses evidence from different sources to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop a thesis that demonstrates knowledgeable judgments.
description_plain: Your child can create a clear main idea that shows they understand the topic well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child will write a conclusion that sums up their information or explanation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic through sustained use of the most significant and relevant facts, concrete details, quotations, or other information from multiple authoritative sources appropriate to the audience’s knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic using important facts, details, quotes, or information from trusted sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adopt an organizational structure that clarifies relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child will organize their writing so ideas and connections are easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly by providing context, presenting well-defined theses, and previewing what follows.
description_plain: Your child can start writing by clearly introducing the topic, giving background, and telling what will come next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child understands different ways to write and the reasons for writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will write in a variety of forms for diverse audiences and purposes linked to grade eleven content and text with an emphasis on argumentative and analytical writing.
description_plain: Your child will write in different styles for various readers, focusing on making strong arguments and analyzing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast informational and technical texts for intent, content, and clarity.
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how informational and technical texts are different or similar in purpose, content, and clarity.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze information within and between paired passages for similar and conflicting ideas and how authors reach similar or different conclusions.
description_plain: Your child will look at two texts to find similar or different ideas and see how the authors come to their conclusions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and analyze the author’s purpose and impact of ambiguity, contradiction, paradox, oxymoron, irony, sarcasm, overstatement, and understatement in informational texts.
description_plain: Your child understands why an author might use unclear or opposite ideas, like irony or sarcasm, and how that affects the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and interpret the key terms (e.g., content-specific words and phrases, technical terminology) and ideas of historical, scientific, technical, and employment texts to clarify concepts.
description_plain: Your child can explain important words and ideas in history, science, or technical texts to better understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how textual elements and organizational patterns contribute to meaning and the author’s purpose.
description_plain: Your child will notice how the way a text is organized helps explain its meaning and the author's purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the relevance and quality of an author’s premises, claims, counterclaims, and evidence by corroborating or challenging them with other information.
description_plain: Your child can judge if an author's reasons and evidence are good by checking them against other information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the hypotheses, data, analysis, and/or conclusions in informational, historical, scientific, or technical texts, verifying the data when possible and corroborating or challenging conclusions with other sources of information.
description_plain: Your child will examine data and conclusions in informational texts and check if they match other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret and complete an application for employment or college admission, and summarize the intent, main ideas, and purpose of the workplace or technical documents.
description_plain: Your child can fill out job or college applications and explain the main ideas and purpose of workplace or technical documents.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands main ideas and supporting details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence from complex informational texts to understand and learn new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how authors’ attitudes, viewpoints, and beliefs reflect larger historical, social, or cultural contexts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's views show the history, society, or culture of their time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Relate themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works to contemporary stories, poems, or drama.
description_plain: Your child will connect themes or characters from myths or traditional stories to modern stories, poems, or plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the influence of the historical and cultural context on form, style, and point of view of texts that represent diverse voices and perspectives.
description_plain: Your child understands how history and culture affect the style and point of view in stories from different voices.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the use of satire, sarcasm, irony, and understatement to differentiate between what is directly stated and what is implied.
description_plain: Your child can recognize when authors use humor or irony to show meanings that are different from what is directly said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Critique how authors use key literary devices (e.g., imagery, personification, symbolism) to contribute to the meaning of a text, including its character development, theme, conflict, and archetypes.
description_plain: Your child will explain how authors use pictures in words, symbols, and other writing tricks to help tell the story and show characters, themes, and conflicts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how authors use specific word choices, syntax, tone, and voice to convey the author’s intent and viewpoint.
description_plain: Your child can notice how an author’s choice of words and sentence style shows their feelings and point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret and analyze how the sound and imagery of poetry support the subject, mood, form, and theme and appeal to the reader’s senses.
description_plain: Your child understands how the sounds and images in poems help create the mood and meaning and make the poem interesting to the senses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and evaluate how dramatic conventions (e.g., soliloquy, aside, narration, direct address to the audience) contribute to the theme and effect of plays from various cultures.
description_plain: Your child can explain how special parts of plays, like characters talking to themselves or the audience, help show the main ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how characters are revealed through particular lines of dialogue or events.
description_plain: Your child will show how characters’ personalities and feelings are revealed through what they say and what happens to them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the setting and plot.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a sentence, chapter, or scene fits into the whole story and helps build the setting and plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the development of universal themes (e.g., loss of innocence, coming of age, relationship with nature) prevalent in American literature (e.g., short stories, poems, plays, novels, essays, and literary nonfiction) of different eras.
description_plain: Your child understands common big ideas like growing up or loss that appear in American stories, poems, and plays from different times.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include literary nonfiction (including world, British, and American literature), narratives, poetry, and drama, with an emphasis on American literature.
description_plain: Your child will use examples from the text to understand and learn from different kinds of stories, poems, and plays, especially American literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in multiple contexts, including in students’ discussions and speaking and writing activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words they learn in speaking and writing in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of figurative language and literary and classical allusions and analyze their role in texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain what figurative language and references to famous stories or history mean and how they add to the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain and analyze idiomatic language in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains phrases that don’t mean exactly what the words say, like idioms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations (e.g., clever, cunning, brainy).
description_plain: Your child can tell the small differences in meaning between words that seem similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use structural analysis of roots, affixes, and etymology to understand the meanings of unfamiliar and complex words.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of words, like roots and prefixes, to figure out the meanings of new or hard words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context and sentence structure to clarify the meanings of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence and surrounding words to understand the meaning of new words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and accurately use general academic and content-specific vocabulary through reading, discussing, and writing about grade-level texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses important academic and subject-specific words by reading, talking, and writing about grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child works on building their vocabulary and understanding of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on grade eleven content and texts.
description_plain: Your child will steadily grow their vocabulary and word knowledge through grade 11 reading and content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to text structure, common organizational structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies, 3-12).
description_plain: Your child uses reading strategies like summarizing and asking questions to understand difficult parts of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.). Use this background knowledge as context for new learning (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads groups of related texts on a topic to build knowledge and vocabulary, reading independently or with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussions and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).V
description_plain: Your child can find and use several pieces of evidence from challenging texts to support their ideas when talking or writing, including quoting or putting information in their own words and showing where they found it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the lower range of the grades 11-12 band to generate and respond logically to literal, inferential, evaluative, synthesizing, and critical thinking questions (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.) (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different kinds of stories and information that are appropriate for 11th grade and answer questions that ask about facts, ideas, opinions, and deeper meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex texts with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can read difficult texts smoothly, accurately, and with expression, checking and fixing any mistakes to understand the meaning better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child will read a variety of challenging texts carefully, read widely to learn new information and words, and use strategies to understand when reading gets hard.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled Readers and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: This is about helping older students become better readers and read for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ethical and responsible use of all sources, including the Internet, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and new technologies, as they develop.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use all sources, including the internet and new technologies like AI, in a responsible and honest way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define the meaning and consequences of plagiarism and follow ethical and legal guidelines for gathering and using information.
description_plain: Your child knows what plagiarism means, why it’s wrong, and follows rules for gathering and using information honestly and legally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite sources for quoted and paraphrased ideas using a standard method of documentation, such as the Modern Language Association (MLA) or the American Psychological Association (APA).
description_plain: Your child can give credit to sources when they quote or explain ideas by using standard formats like MLA or APA.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create research products aligned with the demands of the reading and writing standards.
description_plain: Your child can create research projects that meet the reading and writing expectations for 12th grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize multiple streams of evidence to support claims and acknowledge counterclaims.
description_plain: Your child can combine different pieces of evidence to support their ideas and also recognize opposing viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Objectively evaluate primary and secondary sources for their credibility, reliability, accuracy, usefulness, and limitations; that includes identifying the main and supporting ideas, points of view, conflicting information, and any misconceptions or biases.
description_plain: Your child can look at different sources carefully to decide if they are trustworthy, accurate, useful, and fair, noticing main ideas, opinions, conflicts, and any bias.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather and organize information from various sources.
description_plain: Your child can gather and organize information from many different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate and revise questions about a research topic broadening or narrowing the inquiry as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can ask and change questions about a research topic to make it broader or narrower as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluation and Synthesis of Information
description_plain: Your child will learn how to evaluate and combine information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will conduct research and read a series of conceptually related texts on selected topics to build knowledge on grade-twelve content, texts, and areas prompted by student interest.
description_plain: Your child will research and read related texts on chosen topics to build knowledge and explore areas they are interested in at a 12th-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze media to determine the cause-and-effect relationship(s) between media coverage and public opinion trends.
description_plain: Your child can look at media and explain how it affects what people think and how opinions change.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain and analyze how values and viewpoints are included or excluded and how media influences beliefs, interpretations, and behaviors.
description_plain: Your child can explain how media includes or leaves out certain values and viewpoints and how this influences what people believe and do.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and evaluate how the media's use of symbolism, imagery, metaphor, and bias impacts the message.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how media uses symbols, pictures, comparisons, and bias to affect its message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and critique the effectiveness of media messages by evaluating the purpose, evidence, and clarity for specific purposes with targeted audiences.
description_plain: Your child can evaluate how well media messages work by looking at their purpose, evidence, and clarity for specific audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Defend hypotheses about an author’s underlying values, viewpoints, and purposes and reflect on how they shape the content.
description_plain: Your child can explain an author’s values, viewpoints, and reasons behind their writing and think about how these shape the content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide appropriate citation of all content from external sources.
description_plain: Your child can correctly give credit for all information taken from other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information to create media messages with visual, audio, and graphic components to convey meaning.
description_plain: Your child can organize information and use pictures, sounds, and graphics to create messages that clearly share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize multiple streams of information on the same or similar topic to create a summary or formulate a position.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources on the same topic to make a summary or share their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine multimodal sources’ claims, arguments, ideas, and any point of personal bias by evaluating them for reliability and relevance to serve as credible supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child can look at different types of media and decide if their claims and ideas are trustworthy and important.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create and deliver planned, multimodal, interactive presentations collaboratively and individually for a variety of purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can plan and give presentations using different media, working alone or with others, for different audiences and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrating Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child understands how to use different types of media and communication together to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Memorize and recite an excerpt or monologue from a dramatic work portraying the emotions and motivations of the character through voice and body language that conveys the intended tone and mood of the selection.
description_plain: Your child can memorize and perform a part from a play, showing the character’s feelings and mood with their voice and body.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluating the content and effectiveness of one's presentations, including the introduction, organization, strengths/weaknesses and evidence and reasoning, and conclusion.
description_plain: Your child can think about their own presentations and decide what parts worked well and what could be better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Monitoring audience engagement effectively and adjusting delivery accordingly during presentations.
description_plain: Your child can notice how the audience reacts during a presentation and change how they speak to keep their attention.

subject: Language Arts
description: Anticipating and addressing alternative or opposing perspectives and counterclaims and rebuttals, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can think about other opinions and respond to them during a presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Incorporating various rhetorical devices to enhance purpose and strengthening clarity of ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use different speaking techniques to make their ideas clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choosing appropriate vocabulary, language, and tone for a selected topic, purpose, context, and audience when speaking and presenting.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right words and tone when speaking based on the topic and who they are talking to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Selecting the modes and purposes for presentations and synthesizing multiple streams of information.
description_plain: Your child can decide how to present information and combine details from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an opinion. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can speak about a topic or share their opinion clearly, using different ways to present.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Presentation of Ideas
description_plain: Your child understands how to speak and present ideas clearly to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using reflection to evaluate one's own role in the group process in small group activities.
description_plain: Your child can think about how they worked with others in small groups and what role they played.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluating the content of presentations, including introduction, organization, strengths/weaknesses and evidence and reasoning, and conclusion.
description_plain: Your child can review presentations to see how well they were organized and supported with evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Selecting and applying multimodal tools to design and develop presentation content appropriate for topic and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use different tools to create presentations that fit the topic and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarizing points of agreement and disagreement.
description_plain: Your child can explain where people agree and disagree during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Responding thoughtfully and tactfully with evidence to diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can respond thoughtfully and politely to different opinions using evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Applying a variety of strategies to listen actively and speak purposefully and respectfully.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and speak clearly and respectfully in conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Facilitate and contribute to a range of sustained collaborative discussions with diverse partners on grade twelve topics and texts. This includes:
description_plain: Your child can take part in and help lead thoughtful group discussions on topics and texts for grade twelve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication, Listening, and Collaboration
description_plain: Your child understands how to communicate clearly, listen well, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will develop effective oral communication and collaboration skills to build a community of learners that process, understand, and interpret content together.
description_plain: Your child will practice speaking and working with others to learn and understand ideas together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communication and Multimodal Literacies
description_plain: Your child learns to communicate and understand information using many different ways like speaking, writing, and media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly, consulting reference materials to check as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly and use reference tools to check spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply a style manual, such as that of the Modern Language Association (MLA) or the American Psychological Association (APA), to apply rules for punctuation and formatting of direct quotations in writing.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use style guides like MLA or APA to correctly punctuate and format quotes in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use various clauses (independent, dependent, noun, relative, adverbial) to infuse sentence variety, add interest, and enhance meaning and purpose.
description_plain: Your child uses different types of clauses in sentences to make writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use the conventions of Standard English when speaking and writing, differentiating between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is more appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses proper English rules when speaking and writing and knows when to use formal or informal language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and revise to a standard acceptable both in the workplace and in postsecondary education.
description_plain: Your child can write and improve their work to meet standards needed for jobs and college.

subject: Language Arts
description: Edit writing for appropriate conventions, style, and language in informal and formal contexts. (See Language Usage for grade level expectations)
description_plain: Your child can edit their writing to use correct grammar, style, and language for both casual and formal situations.

subject: Language Arts
