description: Use peer- and self-evaluation to edit writing for clarity and quality of information, addressing strengths and making suggestions regarding how writing might be improved.
description_plain: Your child uses feedback from themselves and others to make their writing clearer and better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing for clarity of content, accuracy, and depth of information.
description_plain: Your child revises their writing to make sure the ideas are clear, accurate, and detailed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Elaborating ideas clearly and effectively through syntactic structure, subordination of ideas, sensory/ concrete details, diction, and purposeful word choice.
description_plain: Your child explains ideas clearly using sentence structure, details, and careful word choice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Embedding narrative techniques and organizing information logically and effectively to guide the audience from one idea to another with transitional words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques and organizes information so readers can follow ideas easily with transition words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Contextualizing evidence from sources effectively with proper introduction and thorough explanation and appropriate citation.
description_plain: Your child introduces and explains evidence from sources clearly and cites it properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Selecting appropriate evidence from multiple texts to clarify ideas, illustrate a counterargument, and/or strengthen a thesis in writing.
description_plain: Your child picks the best evidence from different texts to support ideas, show opposing views, or strengthen their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organizing claims, counterclaims, and evidence in a sustained and logical sequence to exhibit unity.
description_plain: Your child arranges claims, opposing claims, and evidence in a clear and logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Composing a thesis statement that clearly communicates the writer’s position or assertion.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear thesis statement that shows their main opinion or idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and organize writing to address a specific audience and purpose using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing). This includes:
description_plain: Your child plans and organizes writing for a specific audience and purpose by going through steps like planning, drafting, revising, and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend multiple modes of writing, by routinely engaging in the production of shorter and longer pieces that adapt vocabulary, voice, and tone for a range of audiences, purposes, and tasks (e.g., summaries, reflections, descriptions, critiques, letters, poetry, narratives, etc.).
description_plain: Your child writes different kinds of pieces, like summaries, stories, or letters, and changes their words and tone to fit different readers and purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generate technical writing (e.g., job description, questionnaire, job application, or business communication) that clearly address specific audiences with identified purposes.
description_plain: Your child creates clear technical writing, like job descriptions or business letters, aimed at specific audiences and goals.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style matches the intended audience and purpose of the workplace and/or post-secondary education and language in informal and formal contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in a way that fits the audience and purpose, whether for work, college, or casual and formal situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe personal qualifications for potential occupational or educational opportunities.
description_plain: Your child can explain their personal skills and qualities for jobs or educational opportunities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that sums up the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic through sustained use of the most significant and relevant facts, concrete details, quotations, or other information from multiple authoritative sources appropriate to the audience’s knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by using important facts, details, quotes, or information from trusted sources that fit the audience’s knowledge.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adopt an organizational structure that clarifies relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can organize their writing so ideas and concepts are clearly connected and easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly by providing context, presenting well-defined theses, and previewing what follows.
description_plain: Your child can start writing by clearly introducing the topic, giving background, stating their main idea, and previewing what comes next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Modes and Purposes for Writing
description_plain: Your child understands different ways and reasons to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will write in a variety of forms for diverse audiences and purposes linked to grade twelve content and text, with an emphasis on technical writing.
description_plain: Your child will write in many styles for different readers and purposes, focusing especially on technical writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the author organizes an analysis or series of ideas or events, including the order in which the points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections that are drawn between them.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes ideas or events, including the order, how points are introduced and developed, and how they connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate texts with differing points of view on the same or similar events or issues by assessing claims, reasoning, evidence, and connections to other works or historical events.
description_plain: Your child can compare texts with different views on the same topic by checking the claims, reasons, evidence, and links to other works or history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning, author’s attitude toward the subject, and mood.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how specific word choices affect the meaning, the author’s feelings about the topic, and the mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze text structures to discern how they affect the meaning and message of informational and technical writing and how their text structures differ from those in narrative texts.
description_plain: Your child can study how the structure of informational and technical texts affects their meaning and how these structures differ from stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the argument and specific claims in texts, examining whether the reasoning is valid, the evidence is relevant, and whether there are any false premises or unsupported claims.
description_plain: Your child can examine arguments and claims in texts to see if the reasoning makes sense, the evidence fits, and if there are any false or unsupported ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the effectiveness of the structure(s) and rhetorical devices authors use in their exposition or argument, including how the structure advances their point of view.
description_plain: Your child can judge how well an author’s structure and writing techniques support their point of view in explanations or arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret and complete an application for employment or college admission, and summarize the intent, main ideas, and purpose of the workplace or technical documents.
description_plain: Your child can fill out job or college applications and summarize the main ideas and purpose of workplace or technical documents.

subject: Language Arts
description: Key Ideas and Confirming Details
description_plain: Your child understands key ideas and supporting details in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from grade-level complex informational texts read.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence from complex informational texts to understand and learn new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how authors’ attitudes, viewpoints, and beliefs reflect larger historical, social, or cultural contexts.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how an author’s attitudes and beliefs reflect the historical, social, or cultural background.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the development of a universal theme over the course of two or more literary works including how each theme emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details.
description_plain: Your child can compare how a common theme develops in two or more literary works, showing how details shape and change the theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast traditional and contemporary texts that draw on similar themes, patterns of events, or character types with an emphasis on poetry, from various cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare traditional and modern texts from different cultures that share similar themes, events, or characters, especially in poetry.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the use of satire, sarcasm, irony, and understatement to differentiate between what is directly stated and what is implied.
description_plain: Your child can recognize when a story uses satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement to show ideas that are not directly said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret and analyze how authors create intended effects using diction and impact the tone and mood of the story, play, or poem.
description_plain: Your child understands how authors choose words to create feelings and set the mood in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how the use of figurative language in poetry and prose contributes to the reader’s understanding of the subject, form, mood, and theme.
description_plain: Your child can explain how figurative language in poems and stories helps readers understand the topic, mood, and main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and evaluate how dramatic conventions (e.g., soliloquy, aside, narration, direct address to the audience) contribute to the theme and effect of plays from various cultures.
description_plain: Your child can explain how special parts of plays, like soliloquies or talking to the audience, help show the main message and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how complex characters-those with multiple or conflicting motivations-develop over the course of texts, interact with other characters, and advance the plot.
description_plain: Your child understands how complex characters with different feelings and goals change during a story and affect what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how authors structure a text and order events within it through parallel episodes, subplots, and conflicts, and explain how they create such effects as mystery, tension, or surprise.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors organize stories with events, side stories, and conflicts to create surprise, tension, or mystery.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the development of universal themes (e.g., loss of innocence, coming of age, relationship with nature) prevalent in British literature (e.g., short stories, poems, plays, novels, essays, and literary nonfiction) of different eras.
description_plain: Your child can identify common themes like growing up or loss of innocence in British literature from different time periods.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will use textual evidence to demonstrate comprehension and build knowledge from a variety of grade-level complex literary texts read to include literary nonfiction (including world, British and American literature), narratives, poetry, and drama, with an emphasis on British literature.
description_plain: Your child can use details from complex stories, poems, and plays, especially British literature, to understand and learn from them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use newly learned words and phrases in multiple contexts, including in students’ discussions and speaking and writing activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases in speaking and writing in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret the meaning of figurative language and literary and classical allusions and analyze their role in texts.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains the meaning of figurative language and references to famous stories or history in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain and analyze idiomatic language in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain the meaning of common expressions that don’t mean exactly what the words say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations (e.g., assertive, aggressive, domineering).
description_plain: Your child can notice small differences in meaning between similar words, like 'assertive' and 'aggressive.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use structural analysis of roots, affixes, and etymology to understand the meanings of unfamiliar and complex words.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of hard words by looking at their parts, like roots and prefixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context and sentence structure to clarify the meanings of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a difficult word and sentence clues to understand what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and accurately use general academic and content-specific vocabulary through reading, discussing, and writing about grade-level texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses important academic and subject-specific words by reading, talking, and writing about grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vocabulary Development and Word Analysis
description_plain: Your child builds vocabulary and learns how to understand words better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will systematically build vocabulary and word knowledge based on grade twelve content and texts.
description_plain: Your child grows their vocabulary and word knowledge by reading and learning grade 12 materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reading strategies as needed to aid and monitor comprehension when encountering challenging sections of text. These sense-making strategies attend to text structure, common organizational structures, summarizing, asking questions of the text, and others (Reading Strategies, 3-12).
description_plain: Your child uses reading strategies like summarizing and asking questions to understand hard parts of texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading a series of conceptually related texts organized around topics of study to build knowledge and vocabulary (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.). Use this background knowledge as context for new learning. (Deep Reading on Topics to Build Knowledge and Vocabulary, K-12).
description_plain: Your child reads groups of related texts on a topic to build knowledge and learn new words, sometimes with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: When responding to text through discussions and/or writing, draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level complex texts to support claims, conclusions, and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where relevant evidence is located (Textual Evidence, K-12).
description_plain: Your child supports their ideas in discussions and writing by using several pieces of evidence from complex texts, including quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Proficiently read and comprehend a variety of literary and informational texts that exhibit complexity at the higher range of the grades 11-12 band to generate and respond logically to literal, inferential, evaluative, synthesizing, and critical thinking questions (See the Quantitative and Qualitative Analysis charts for determining complexity in the Appendix.). (Text Complexity, 2-12).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information, and answer different types of questions about what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of grade-level complex text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and meaningful expression in successive readings to support comprehension. Monitor while reading to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, as necessary (Reading Fluency, K-12).
description_plain: Your child can read hard texts smoothly and clearly, and check their reading to fix mistakes or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student will build knowledge and comprehension skills from reading a range of challenging, content-rich texts. This includes fluently reading and gathering evidence from grade-level complex texts, reading widely on topics to gain purposeful knowledge and vocabulary, and using reading strategies when comprehension breaks down.
description_plain: Your child will read challenging texts to learn new things, find proof in the text, and use strategies to understand when reading gets hard.

subject: Language Arts
description: Developing Skilled and Building Reading Stamina
description_plain: Your child is learning to read well and build the ability to read for longer periods.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying the geographic location of the United States and Virginia on a map and globe
description_plain: Your child can find the United States and Virginia on a map or globe.

subject: Social Studies
description: telling the stories of the people that developed their local communities
description_plain: Your child can tell stories about the people who helped build their local community.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying people who helped establish and lead the local community over time
description_plain: Your child can name people who helped start and lead their local community over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying examples of historic events, stories, and legends
description_plain: Your child can recognize important historic events, stories, and legends.

subject: Social Studies
description: recognizing the Pledge of Allegiance and the National Anthem
description_plain: Your child knows the Pledge of Allegiance and the National Anthem.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying national and state symbols, including, but not limited to the bald eagle, the dogwood, and the cardinal
description_plain: Your child can identify national and state symbols like the bald eagle, dogwood flower, and cardinal bird.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying the American and Virginia flags
description_plain: Your child can recognize the American flag and the Virginia state flag.

subject: Social Studies
description: George Washington Day (commonly known as Presidents’ Day)
description_plain: Your child learns about George Washington Day, also called Presidents’ Day.

subject: Social Studies
description: taking turns, sharing, and working well with others for the good of everyone
description_plain: Your child practices taking turns, sharing, and working well with others.

subject: Social Studies
description: using content vocabulary to demonstrate comprehension
description_plain: Your child uses new words to show they understand what they learn.

subject: Social Studies
description: practicing civility, respect, hard work, and responsible citizenship skills
description_plain: Your child practices being respectful, working hard, and being a responsible citizen.

subject: Social Studies
description: using economic decision-making models to make informed economic decisions
description_plain: Your child learns to make smart choices about money and resources.

subject: Social Studies
description: recognizing cause-and-effect relationships
description_plain: Your child can see how one event causes another to happen.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying similarities and differences to explain content
description_plain: Your child can find ways things are alike and different to explain ideas.

subject: Social Studies
description: sequencing and organizing information
description_plain: Your child can put events or information in the right order.

subject: Social Studies
description: demonstrating curiosity and critical thinking through questioning
description_plain: Your child shows curiosity and thinks carefully by asking questions.

subject: Social Studies
description: applying geographic skills to identify and understand geographic features
description_plain: Your child can use simple map skills to find and understand different places and land features.

subject: Social Studies
description: viewing and exploring information sources, including, but not limited to artifacts, primary and secondary sources, charts, graphs, and diagrams
description_plain: Your child will look at and explore different types of information like pictures, charts, and objects to learn about the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will apply history and social science skills to the content by
description_plain: Your child understands basic history and social studies ideas by practicing related skills.

subject: Social Studies
description: describing why people must make choices, prioritizing needs over wants
description_plain: Your child can explain why people have to make choices and why needs are more important than wants.

subject: Social Studies
description: describing goods and services produced in Virginia
description_plain: Your child can name some goods and services that are made or provided in Virginia.

subject: Social Studies
description: locating Richmond and Washington, D.C., on Virginia and United States maps
description_plain: Your child can find Richmond and Washington, D.C., on maps of Virginia and the United States.

subject: Social Studies
description: constructing simple maps and including a title, map legend or key, and compass rose
description_plain: Your child can draw simple maps that include a title, a key to explain symbols, and a compass showing directions.

subject: Social Studies
description: locating Virginia, the United States, continents, and oceans on maps and globes
description_plain: Your child can find Virginia, the United States, continents, and oceans on maps and globes.

subject: Social Studies
description: Barrier Breakers: John Mercer Langston, Booker T. Washington, Maggie L. Walker, Barbara Johns, Arthur R. Ashe, Jr., and L. Douglas Wilder
description_plain: Your child will learn about important people like John Mercer Langston and Maggie L. Walker who helped break barriers.

subject: Social Studies
description: explaining why people use symbols and have traditions
description_plain: Your child can explain why people use symbols and have traditions to show what they believe and value.

subject: Social Studies
description: George Washington Day (commonly known as Presidents’ Day)
description_plain: Your child will learn about George Washington Day, also called Presidents’ Day.

subject: Social Studies
description: understanding that local and state government representatives are elected by citizens who vote
description_plain: Your child understands that people vote to choose leaders in local and state government.

subject: Social Studies
description: contributing one’s time and talents to help others in their homes, schools, and communities through jobs
description_plain: Your child can help others by using their time and skills at home, school, and in the community.

subject: Social Studies
description: using content vocabulary to demonstrate comprehension
description_plain: Your child can use important words from social studies to show they understand what they learn.

subject: Social Studies
description: practicing civility, respect, hard work, and responsible citizenship skills
description_plain: Your child practices being polite, respectful, working hard, and acting responsibly as a citizen.

subject: Social Studies
description: using economic decision-making models to make informed economic decisions
description_plain: Your child can use simple ways to make good choices about money and resources.

subject: Social Studies
description: recognizing cause-and-effect relationships
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain how one event can cause another to happen.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying similarities and differences to clarify and explain content
description_plain: Your child can find and explain how things are alike and different to better understand ideas.

subject: Social Studies
description: sequencing and organizing information
description_plain: Your child can put events or information in the right order to make sense of it.

subject: Social Studies
description: demonstrating curiosity and critical thinking through questioning
description_plain: Your child shows curiosity and thinks carefully by asking questions.

subject: Social Studies
description: applying geographic skills to identify and understand geographic features
description_plain: Your child can use simple map skills to find and understand different land features like mountains, rivers, and lakes.

subject: Social Studies
description: viewing and exploring information sources, including, but not limited to artifacts, primary and secondary sources, charts, graphs, and diagrams
description_plain: Your child will look at and explore different types of information like old objects, pictures, charts, and stories to learn about the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: The student will apply history and social science skills to the content by
description_plain: Your child understands how to use history and social studies skills to learn about people and events.

subject: Social Studies
description: explaining that scarcity (limited resources) requires people to make choices about producing and consuming goods and services
description_plain: Your child can explain that because resources are limited, people have to make choices about what to make and use.

subject: Social Studies
description: distinguishing between the use of barter and the use of money in the exchange for goods and services and describing how money makes trading easier than barter
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between trading goods directly (barter) and using money, and explain why money makes trading easier.

subject: Social Studies
description: distinguishing between needs and wants
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between things people need to live and things they want for fun or comfort.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying natural resources (e.g., water, soil, wood, coal), human resources (i.e., people at work), and capital resources (e.g., machines, tools, computers, buildings)
description_plain: Your child can name natural resources like water and wood, people who work (human resources), and tools or machines (capital resources).

subject: Social Studies
description: Valley Forge and George Washington’s crossing of the Delaware River
description_plain: Your child will learn about important events like Valley Forge and when George Washington crossed the Delaware River.

subject: Social Studies
description: Jefferson Memorial and Lincoln Memorial
description_plain: Your child will learn about the Jefferson Memorial and Lincoln Memorial and why they are important.

subject: Social Studies
description: identifying major rivers, mountain ranges, lakes, and other physical features in the United States
description_plain: Your child can find major rivers, mountains, lakes, and other land features in the United States on a map.

subject: Social Studies
description: locating the countries of North America and the bordering oceans
description_plain: Your child can find the countries in North America and the oceans that touch them on a map.

subject: Social Studies
description: Reformers and Champions: Abraham Lincoln, Theodore “Teddy” Roosevelt, Helen Keller, Thurgood Marshall, Rosa Parks, Jackie Robinson, Cesar Chavez, Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., Sen. Daniel Inouye, John F. Kennedy, and Barack Obama
description_plain: Your child will learn about important people who worked for change, like Abraham Lincoln, Rosa Parks, and Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explorers and Adventurers: Meriwether Lewis, William Clark, and Sacagawea, Neil Armstrong, Amelia Earhart
description_plain: Your child will learn about explorers and adventurers like Lewis and Clark, Neil Armstrong, and Amelia Earhart.

subject: Social Studies
description: Scholars and Inventors: Benjamin Franklin, Benjamin Banneker, Thomas Jefferson, George Washington Carver, Booker T. Washington, Orville and Wilbur Wright, Steve Jobs, Jonas Salk, Thomas Edison, Alexander Graham Bell, and Mary Jackson
description_plain: Your child will learn about inventors and scholars like Benjamin Franklin, the Wright brothers, and Thomas Edison.

subject: Social Studies
description: describing the impact of the Louisiana Purchase on the westward expansion of the United States
description_plain: Your child can explain how the Louisiana Purchase helped the United States grow westward.

subject: Social Studies
description: recognizing the reasons for moving the nation’s capital from Philadelphia to Washington, D.C., and the importance of the nation’s capital
description_plain: Your child understands why the nation’s capital moved from Philadelphia to Washington, D.C., and why the capital is important.

subject: Social Studies
description: George Washington (“Father of our Country”)
description_plain: Your child will learn about George Washington and why he is called the 'Father of our Country.'

subject: Social Studies
description: James Madison (“Father of the Constitution”)
description_plain: Your child will learn about James Madison and why he is called the 'Father of the Constitution.'

subject: Social Studies
description: understanding Tribal Sovereignty, including but not limited to the importance of land, history, and culture
description_plain: Your child understands Tribal Sovereignty and why land, history, and culture are important to Native American tribes.

subject: Social Studies
description: learning about the meaning of the American flag and the words chosen in the Pledge of Allegiance and the National Anthem
